verse.language: Tobit was from the tribe and city of Naphtali (which is in the upper parts of Galilee above Asher, after the way, which leads to the west, that has on its left the city of Sephet).
Tobit was from the tribe and city of Naphtali (which is in the upper parts of Galilee above Asher, after the way, which leads to the west, that has on its left the city of Sephet).
كَانَ طُوبِيَّا وَهُوَ مِنْ سِبْطِ وَمَدِينَةِ نَفْتَالِي الَّتِي فِي الْجَلِيلِ الأَعْلَى فَوْقَ نَحْشُونَ وَرَاءَ الطَّرِيقِ الآخِذِ غَرْباً وَإِلَى يَسَارِهَا مَدِينَةُ صَفَتَ،
Although he had been taken captive in the days of Shalmaneser, the king of the Assyrians, even in such a situation as captivity, he did not desert the way of truth.
Although he had been taken captive in the days of Shalmaneser, the king of the Assyrians, even in such a situation as captivity, he did not desert the way of truth.
قَدْ جُلِيَ فِي عَهْدِ شَلْمَنْأَسَرَ مَلِكِ أَشُّورَ إِلاَّ أَنَّهُ مَعَ كَوْنِهِ فِي الْجَلاَءِ لَمْ يُفَارِقْ سَبِيلَ الْحَقِّ،
So then, every day, all that he was able to obtain, he bestowed on his fellow captive brothers, who were from his kindred.
So then, every day, all that he was able to obtain, he bestowed on his fellow captive brothers, who were from his kindred.
حَتَّى كَانَ كُلُّ مَا يَتَيَسَّرُ لَهُ يَقْسِمُهُ كُلَّ يَوْمٍ عَلَى مَنْ جُلِيَ مَعَهُ مِنْ إِخْوَانِهِ الَّذِينَ مِنْ جِنْسِهِ.
And, when he was among the youngest of any in the tribe of Naphtali, he showed not so much as any childish behavior in his work.
And, when he was among the youngest of any in the tribe of Naphtali, he showed not so much as any childish behavior in his work.
وَمَعَ أَنَّهُ كَانَ أَحْدَثَ الْجَمِيعِ فِي سِبْطِ نَفْتَالِيَ لَمْ يَكُنْ عَلَى شَيْءٍ مِنْ شُؤُونِ الأَحْدَاثِ.
And then, when all went to the golden calves which Jeroboam, king of Israel, had made, he alone fled from the company of them all.
And then, when all went to the golden calves which Jeroboam, king of Israel, had made, he alone fled from the company of them all.
وَكَانَ إِذَا قَصَدُوا كُلُّهُمْ عُجُولَ الذَّهَبِ الَّتِي عَمِلَهَا يَارُبْعَامُ مَلِكُ إِسْرَائِيلَ، يَتَخَلَّفُ وَحْدَهُ عَنْ سَائِرِهِمْ
Yet he continued on to Jerusalem, to the temple of the Lord, and there he adored the Lord God of Israel, offering faithfully all his first-fruits and his tithes.
Yet he continued on to Jerusalem, to the temple of the Lord, and there he adored the Lord God of Israel, offering faithfully all his first-fruits and his tithes.
فَيَمْضِي إِلَى أُورُشَلِيمَ إِلَى هَيْكَلِ الرَّبِّ، وَهُنَاكَ كَانَ يَسْجُدُ لِلرَّبِّ إِلهِ إِسْرَائِيلَ، وَيُوفِي جَمِيعَ بَوَاكِيرِهِ وَأَعْشَارِهِ.
So then, in the third year, he administered all his tithes to new converts and to new arrivals.
So then, in the third year, he administered all his tithes to new converts and to new arrivals.
وَإِذَا كَانَتِ السَّنَةُ الثَّالِثَةُ كَانَ يَجْعَلُ جَمِيعَ أَعْشَارِهِ لِلدُّخَلاَءِ وَالْغُرَبَاءِ.
These and similar such things, even as a boy, he observed according to the law of God.
These and similar such things, even as a boy, he observed according to the law of God.
وَعَلَى هذَا وَأَمْثَالِهِ، كَانَ مُثَابِراً مُنْذُ صَبْوَتِهِ عَلَى وَفْقِ شَرِيعَةِ اللهِ.
Truly, when he had become a man, he received as wife Anna of his own tribe, and he conceived a son by her, to whom he assigned his own name.
Truly, when he had become a man, he received as wife Anna of his own tribe, and he conceived a son by her, to whom he assigned his own name.
وَلَمَّا أَنْ صَارَ رَجُلاً، اتَّخَذَ لَهُ امْرَأَةً مِنْ سِبْطِهِ اسْمُهَا حَنَّةُ، فَوُلِدَ لَهُ مِنْهَا وَلَدٌ فَسَمَّاهُ بِاسْمِهِ،
From his infancy, he taught him to fear God and to abstain from all sin.
From his infancy, he taught him to fear God and to abstain from all sin.
وَأَدَّبَهُ مُنْذُ صِغَرِهِ عَلَى تَقْوَى اللهِ وَاجْتِنَابِ كُلِّ خَطِيئَةٍ.
Therefore, when, during the captivity, he had arrived with his wife and son at the city of Nineveh, with all his tribe,
Therefore, when, during the captivity, he had arrived with his wife and son at the city of Nineveh, with all his tribe,
وَلَمَّا جُلِيَ مَعَ امْرَأَتِهِ وَوَلَدِهِ إِلَى مَدِينَةِ نِينَوَى، حَيْثُ كَانَتْ كُلُّ عَشِيرَتِهِ،
(even though they all ate from the foods of the Gentiles,) he guarded his soul and never was contaminated with their foods.
(even though they all ate from the foods of the Gentiles,) he guarded his soul and never was contaminated with their foods.
وَقَدْ كَانُوا كُلُّهُمْ يَأْكُلُونَ مِنْ أَطْعِمَةِ الأُمَمِ، كَانَ هُوَ يَصُونُ نَفْسَهُ وَلَمْ يَتَنَجَّسْ قَطُّ بِمَأْكُولاَتِهِمْ.
And because he was mindful of the Lord with his whole heart, God gave him favor in the sight of Shalmaneser the king.
And because he was mindful of the Lord with his whole heart, God gave him favor in the sight of Shalmaneser the king.
وَلأَجْلِ أَنَّهُ كَانَ يَذْكُرُ الرَّبَّ بِكُلِّ قَلْبِهِ، أَتَاهُ اللهُ حُظْوَةً لَدَى الْمَلِكِ شَلْمَنْأَسَرَ،
And he gave him the power to go wherever he would want, having the freedom to do whatever he wished.
And he gave him the power to go wherever he would want, having the freedom to do whatever he wished.
فَأَطْلَقَ لَهُ أَنْ يَذْهَبَ حَيْثُمَا شَاءَ وَيَفْعَلَ مَا يُرِيدُ.
Therefore, he continued on to all who were in captivity, and he gave them helpful advice.
Therefore, he continued on to all who were in captivity, and he gave them helpful advice.
فَكَانَ يَطُوفُ عَلَى كُلِّ مَنْ كَانَ فِي الْجَلاَءِ، وَيُرْشِدُهُمْ بِنَصَائِحِ الْخَلاَصِ.
But when he had arrived at Rages, a city of the Medes, he had ten talents of silver, from that which he had been given in honor by the king.
But when he had arrived at Rages, a city of the Medes, he had ten talents of silver, from that which he had been given in honor by the king.
ثُمَّ إِنَّهُ قَدِمَ رَاجِيسَ مَدِينَةَ مَادَايَ، وَكَانَ مَعَهُ مِمَّا آثَرَهُ بِهِ الْمَلِكُ عَشَرَةُ قَنَاطِيرَ مِنَ الْفِضَّةِ.
And when, in the midst of the great tumult of his kindred, he saw the destitution of Gabael, who was from his tribe, he loaned him, under a written agreement, the aforementioned weight of silver.
And when, in the midst of the great tumult of his kindred, he saw the destitution of Gabael, who was from his tribe, he loaned him, under a written agreement, the aforementioned weight of silver.
فَرَأَى بَيْنَ الْجُمْهُورِ الْغَفِيرِ الَّذِي مِنْ جِنْسِهِ رَجُلاً مِنْ سِبْطِهِ يُقَالُ لَهُ غَابِيلُوسُ فِي فَاقَةٍ، فَدَفَعَ إِلَيْهِ الزِّنَةَ الْمَذْكُورَةَ مِنَ الْفِضَّةِ بِصَكٍّ.
In truth, after a long time, Shalmaneser the king died, while Sennacherib his son reigned in his place, and he held a hatred for the sons of Israel.
In truth, after a long time, Shalmaneser the king died, while Sennacherib his son reigned in his place, and he held a hatred for the sons of Israel.
وَكَانَ بَعْدَ أَيَّامٍ كَثِيرَةٍ أَنْ مَاتَ الْمَلِكُ شَلْمَنْأَسَرُ فَمَلَكَ سَنْحَارِيبُ ابْنُهُ مَكَانَهُ، فَوَقَعَ بَنُو إِسْرَائِيلَ عِنْدَهُ مَوْقِعَ الْكَرَاهَةِ.
Every day, Tobit traveled through all his own people, and he consoled them, and he distributed to each one as much as he could from his resources.
Every day, Tobit traveled through all his own people, and he consoled them, and he distributed to each one as much as he could from his resources.
وَكَانَ طُوبِيَّا يَطُوفُ كُلَّ يَوْمٍ عَلَى جَمِيعِ عَشِيرَتِهِ وَيُعَزِّيهِمْ، وَيُؤَاسِي كُلَّ وَاحِدٍ مِنْ أَمْوَالِهِ عَلَى قَدْرِ وُسْعِهِ.
He nourished the hungry, and he supplied clothes to the naked, and he showed concern for the burial of the dead and of the slain.
He nourished the hungry, and he supplied clothes to the naked, and he showed concern for the burial of the dead and of the slain.
فَيُطْعِمُ الْجِيَاعَ، وَيَكْسُو الْعُرَاةَ، وَيَدْفِنُ الْمَوْتَى وَالْقَتْلَى بِغَيْرَةٍ شَدِيدَةٍ.

And then, when king Sennacherib had returned from Judea, fleeing the scourge which God had caused all around him because of his blasphemy, and, being angry, he was slaughtering many from the sons of Israel, Tobit buried their bodies.

And then, when king Sennacherib had returned from Judea, fleeing the scourge which God had caused all around him because of his blasphemy, and, being angry, he was slaughtering many from the sons of Israel, Tobit buried their bodies.

وَلَمَّا قَفَلَ الْمَلِكُ سَنْحَارِيبُ مِنْ أَرْضِ يَهُوذَا هَارِباً مِنَ الضَّرْبَةِ الَّتِي حَاقَهُ اللهُ بِهَا بِسَبَبِ تَجْدِيفِهِ، وَطَفِقَ لِحَنَقِهِ يَقْتُلُ كَثِيرِينَ مِنْ بَنِي إِسْرَائِيلَ؛ كَانَ طُوبِيَّا يدْفِنُ أَجْسَادَهُمْ.
And when it was reported to the king, he ordered him to be slain, and he took away all his belongings.
And when it was reported to the king, he ordered him to be slain, and he took away all his belongings.
فَنَمَى ذلِكَ إِلَى الْمَلِكِ، فَأَمَرَ بِقَتْلِهِ وَضَبَطَ جَمِيعَ مَالِهِ.
In truth, Tobit, fleeing with nothing but his son and his wife, was able to remain hidden because many loved him.
In truth, Tobit, fleeing with nothing but his son and his wife, was able to remain hidden because many loved him.
فَهَرَبَ طُوبِيَّا بِوَلَدِهِ وَزَوْجَتِهِ عَارِياً وَاخْتَبَأَ لأَنَّ كَثِيرِينَ كَانُوا يُحِبُّونَهُ.
In truth, after forty-five days, the king was slain by his own sons,
In truth, after forty-five days, the king was slain by his own sons,
وَكَانَ بَعْدَ خَمْسَةٍ وَأَرْبَعِينَ يَوْماً أَنْ قَتَلَ الْمَلِكَ ابْنَاهُ،
and Tobit was able to return to his house, and all his resources were restored to him.
and Tobit was able to return to his house, and all his resources were restored to him.
فَعَادَ طُوبِيَّا إِلَى مَنْزِلِهِ، وَرُدَّ عَلَيْهِ كُلُّ مَالِهِ.
In truth, after this, when there was a feast day of the Lord, and a good dinner had been prepared in the house of Tobit,
In truth, after this, when there was a feast day of the Lord, and a good dinner had been prepared in the house of Tobit,
وَكَانَ بَعْدَ ذلِكَ فِي يَوْمِ عِيدِ الرَّبِّ أَنْ صُنِعَتْ مَأْدُبَةٌ عَظِيمَةٌ فِي بَيْتِ طُوبِيَّا.
he said to his son: “Go, and bring some others who fear God from our tribe to feast with us.”
he said to his son: “Go, and bring some others who fear God from our tribe to feast with us.”
فَقَالَ لاِبْنِهِ: «هَلُمَّ فَادْعُ بَعْضاً مِنْ سِبْطِنَا مِنَ الْمُتَّقِينَ للهِ لِيَأْكُلُوا مَعَنَا».

And after he had gone, returning, he reported to him that one of the sons of Israel, with his throat cut, was lying in the street. And immediately, he leapt from his place reclining at table, left behind his dinner, and went forth with fasting to the body.

And after he had gone, returning, he reported to him that one of the sons of Israel, with his throat cut, was lying in the street. And immediately, he leapt from his place reclining at table, left behind his dinner, and went forth with fasting to the body.

فَانْطَلَقَ ثُمَّ عَادَ فَأَخْبَرَهُ أَنَّ وَاحِداً مِنْ بَنِي إِسْرَائِيلَ مَذْبُوحٌ مُلْقًى فِي السُّوقِ. فَلَمَّا سَمِعَ طُوبِيَّا، نَهَضَ مِنْ مَوْضِعِهِ مُسْرِعاً، وَتَرَكَ الْعَشَاءَ، وَبَلَغَ الْجُثَّةَ وَهُوَ صَائِمٌ،
And taking it up, he carried it in secret to his house, so that, after the sun had set, he might bury him cautiously.
And taking it up, he carried it in secret to his house, so that, after the sun had set, he might bury him cautiously.
فَرَفَعَهَا وَحَمَلَهَا إِلَى بَيْتِهِ سِرًّا لِيَدْفِنَهَا بِالتَّحَفُّظِ بَعْدَ مَغِيبِ الشَّمْسِ.
And after he had hidden the body, he chewed his bread with mourning and fear,
And after he had hidden the body, he chewed his bread with mourning and fear,
وَبَعْدَ أَنْ خَبَأَ الْجُثَّةَ أَكَلَ الطَّعَامَ بَاكِياً مُرْتَعِداً.
remembering the word that the Lord spoke through the prophet Amos: “Your feast days shall be turned into lamentation and mourning.”
remembering the word that the Lord spoke through the prophet Amos: “Your feast days shall be turned into lamentation and mourning.”
فَذَكَرَ الْكَلاَمَ الَّذِي تَكَلَّمَ بِهِ الرَّبُّ عَلَى لِسَانِ عَامُوسَ النَّبِيِّ: «أَيَّامُ أَعْيَادِكُمْ تَتَحَوَّلُ إِلَى عَوِيلٍ وَنَحِيبٍ».
Truly, when the sun had set, he went out, and he buried him.
Truly, when the sun had set, he went out, and he buried him.
وَلَمَّا غَرَبَتِ الشَّمْسُ، ذَهَبَ وَدَفَنَهَا.
Yet all his neighbors argued with him, saying: “Now, an order was given to execute you because of this matter, and you barely escaped a death sentence, and again you are burying the dead?”
Yet all his neighbors argued with him, saying: “Now, an order was given to execute you because of this matter, and you barely escaped a death sentence, and again you are burying the dead?”
وَكَانَ جَمِيعُ ذَوِي قَرَابَتِهِ يَلُومُونَهُ قَائِلِينَ: «لأَجْلِ هذَا أُمِرَ بِقَتْلِكَ، وَمَا كِدْتَ تَنْجُو مِنْ قَضَاءِ الْمَوْتِ حَتَّى عُدْتَ تَدْفِنُ الْمَوْتَى».
But Tobit, fearing God more than the king, stole away the bodies of the slain and concealed them in his house, and in the middle of the night, he buried them.
But Tobit, fearing God more than the king, stole away the bodies of the slain and concealed them in his house, and in the middle of the night, he buried them.

وَأَمَّا طُوبِيَّا، فَإِذْ كَانَ خَوْفُهُ مِنَ اللهِ أَعْظَمَ مِنْ خَوْفِهِ مِنَ الْمَلِكِ، كَانَ لاَ يَزَالُ يَخْطَفُ جُثَثَ الْقَتْلَى وَيَخْبَأُهَا فِي بَيْتِهِ، فَيَدْفِنُهَا عِنْدَ انْتِصَافِ اللَّيْلِ.
But it happened one day, being tired from burying the dead, he came into his house, and he threw himself down next to the wall, and he slept.
But it happened one day, being tired from burying the dead, he came into his house, and he threw himself down next to the wall, and he slept.
وَاتَّفَقَ فِي بَعْضِ الأَيَّامِ، وَقَدْ تَعِبَ مِنْ دَفْنِ الْمَوْتَى، أَنَّهُ وَافَى بَيْتَهُ، فَرَمَى بِنَفْسِهِ إِلَى جَانِبِ الْحَائِطِ وَنَامَ.
And, as he was sleeping, warm droppings from a swallow’s nest fell upon his eyes, and he was made blind.
And, as he was sleeping, warm droppings from a swallow’s nest fell upon his eyes, and he was made blind.
فَوَقَعَ ذَرْقٌ مِنْ عُشِّ خُطَّافٍ فِي عَيْنَيْهِ وَهُوَ سُخْنٌ فَعَمِيَ.
And so the Lord permitted this trial to befall him, in order that an example might be given to posterity of his patience, which is even like that of holy Job.
And so the Lord permitted this trial to befall him, in order that an example might be given to posterity of his patience, which is even like that of holy Job.
وَإِنَّمَا أَذِنَ الرَّبُّ أَنْ تَعْرِضَ لَهُ هذِهِ التَّجْرِبَةُ لِتَكُونَ لِمَنْ بَعْدَهُ قُدْوَةَ صَبْرِهِ كَأَيُّوبَ الصِّدِّيقِ.
For, even from his infancy, he had always feared God and kept his commandments, so he was not discouraged before God because of the scourge of blindness that had befallen him.
For, even from his infancy, he had always feared God and kept his commandments, so he was not discouraged before God because of the scourge of blindness that had befallen him.
فَإِنَّهُ إِذْ كَانَ لَمْ يَنْفَكَّ عَنْ تَقْوَى اللهِ مُنْذُ صِغَرِهِ وَحَافِظاً لِوَصَايَاهُ، لَمْ يَكُنْ يَتَذَمَّرُ عَلَى اللهِ لِمَا نَالَهُ مِنْ بَلْوَى الْعَمَى،
But he remained immoveable in the fear of God, giving thanks to God all the days of his life.
But he remained immoveable in the fear of God, giving thanks to God all the days of his life.
وَلكِنَّهُ ثَبَتَ فِي خَوْفِ اللهِ، شَاكِراً لَهُ طُولَ أَيَّامِ حَيَاتِهِ.
For just as kings have mocked blessed Job, so also his relatives and acquaintances ridiculed his life, saying:
For just as kings have mocked blessed Job, so also his relatives and acquaintances ridiculed his life, saying:
وَكَمَا كَانَ الْقِدِّيسُ أَيُّوبُ يُعَيِّرُهُ الْمُلُوكُ، كَانَ أَنْسِبَاءُ هذَا وَذَوُوهُ يَسْخَرُونَ مِنْ عِيشَتِهِ قَائِلِينَ:
“Where is your hope, on behalf of which you gave alms and buried the dead?”
“Where is your hope, on behalf of which you gave alms and buried the dead?”
«أَيْنَ رَجَاؤُكَ الَّذِي لأَجْلِهِ كُنْتَ تَبْذُلُ الصَّدَقَاتِ وَتَدْفِنُ الْمَوْتَى؟»
In truth, Tobit corrected them, saying: “Do not speak in this way,
In truth, Tobit corrected them, saying: “Do not speak in this way,
فَيَزْجُرُهُمْ طُوبِيَّا قَائِلاً: «لاَ تَتَكَلَّمُوا كَذَا،
for we are the sons of the holy ones, and we look forward to that life which God will give to those who never change in their faith before him.”
for we are the sons of the holy ones, and we look forward to that life which God will give to those who never change in their faith before him.”
فَإِنَّمَا نَحْنُ بَنُو الْقِدِّيسِينَ، وَإِنَّمَا نَنْتَظِرُ تِلْكَ الْحَيَاةَ الَّتِي يَهَبُهَا اللهُ لِلَّذِينَ لاَ يَصْرِفُونَ إِيمَانَهُمْ عَنْهُ أَبَداً».
In truth, his wife Anna went out to weaving work daily, and she brought back the provisions that she was able to obtain by the labor of her hands.
In truth, his wife Anna went out to weaving work daily, and she brought back the provisions that she was able to obtain by the labor of her hands.
وَكَانَتْ حَنَّةُ امْرَأَتُهُ تَذْهَبُ كُلَّ يَوْمٍ إِلَى الْحَاكَةِ وَتَأْتِي مِنْ تَعَبِ يَدَيْهَا بِمَا يَتَأَتَّى لَهَا تَحْصِيلُهُ مِنَ الْمِيرَةِ.
Whereupon it happened that, having received a young goat, she brought it home.
Whereupon it happened that, having received a young goat, she brought it home.
وَاتَّفَقَ أَنَّهَا أَخَذَتْ جَدْياً وَحَمَلَتْهُ إِلَى الْبَيْتِ،
When her husband heard the sound of its bleating, he said, “Look, so that it might not be stolen, return it to its owners, for it is not lawful for us either to eat, or to touch, anything stolen.”
When her husband heard the sound of its bleating, he said, “Look, so that it might not be stolen, return it to its owners, for it is not lawful for us either to eat, or to touch, anything stolen.”

فَلَمَّا سَمِعَ بَعْلُهَا صَوْتَ ثُغَاءِ الْجَدْيَ قَالَ: «انْظُرُوا لَعَلَّهُ يَكُونُ مَسْرُوقاً فَرُدُّوهُ عَلَى أَرْبَابِهِ، إِذْ لاَ يَحِلُّ لَنَا أَنْ نَأْكُلَ وَلاَ نَلْمُسَ شَيْئاً مَسْرُوقاً».
At this, his wife, being angry, answered, “Clearly, your hope has become vanity, and the manner of your almsgiving has become apparent.”
At this, his wife, being angry, answered, “Clearly, your hope has become vanity, and the manner of your almsgiving has become apparent.”
فَأَجَابَتْهُ امْرَأَتُهُ وَهِيَ مُغْضَبَةٌ: «قَدْ وَضَحَ بُطْلاَنُ رَجَائِكَ، وَصَدَقَاتُكَ الآنَ قَدْ عُرِفَتْ».
And with these and other similar such words, she reproached him.
And with these and other similar such words, she reproached him.
وَبِهذَا الْكَلاَمِ وَمِثْلِهِ كَانَتْ تُعَيِّرُهُ.
Then Tobit sighed, and he began to pray with tears,
Then Tobit sighed, and he began to pray with tears,
حِينَئِذٍ أَنَّ طُوبِيَّا وَطَفِقَ يُصَلِّي بِدُمُوعٍ
saying, “O Lord, you are just and all your judgments are just, and all your ways are mercy, and truth, and judgment.
saying, “O Lord, you are just and all your judgments are just, and all your ways are mercy, and truth, and judgment.
وَقَالَ: «عَادِلٌ أَنْتَ أَيُّهَا الرَّبُّ وَجَمِيعُ أَحْكَامِكَ مُسْتَقِيمَةٌ وَطُرْقُكَ كُلُّهَا رَحْمَةٌ وَحَقٌّ وَحُكْمٌ.
And now, O Lord, remember me, and do not take vengeance for my sins, and do not call to mind my offenses, nor those of my parents.
And now, O Lord, remember me, and do not take vengeance for my sins, and do not call to mind my offenses, nor those of my parents.
فَالآنَ اذْكُرْنِي يَا رَبِّ، وَلاَ تَنْتَقِمْ عَنْ خَطَايَايَ، وَلاَ تَذْكُرْ ذُنُوبِي وَلاَ ذُنُوبَ آبَائِي،

For we have not obeyed your precepts, and so we have been handed over to plundering and to captivity, and to death, and to mockery, and as a disgrace before all the nations, among which you have dispersed us.

For we have not obeyed your precepts, and so we have been handed over to plundering and to captivity, and to death, and to mockery, and as a disgrace before all the nations, among which you have dispersed us.
لأَنَّا لَمْ نُطِعْ أَوَامِرَكَ، فَلأَجْلِ ذلِكَ أُسْلِمْنَا إِلَى النَّهْبِ وَالْجَلاَءِ وَالْمَوْتِ، وَأَصْبَحْنَا أُحْدُوثَةً وَعَاراً فِي جَمِيعِ الأُمَمِ الَّتِي بَدَّدْتَنَا بَيْنَهَا.
And now, O Lord, great are your judgments. For we have not acted according to your precepts, and we have not walked sincerely before you.
And now, O Lord, great are your judgments. For we have not acted according to your precepts, and we have not walked sincerely before you.
فَالآنَ يَا رَبِّ، عَظِيمَةٌ أَحْكَامُكَ، لأَنَّا لَمْ نَعْمَلْ بِحَسَبِ وَصَايَاكَ، وَلاَ سَلَكْنَا بِخُلُوصٍ أَمَامَكَ.
And now, O Lord, do with me according to your will, and order my spirit to be received in peace. For it is more expedient for me to die, than to live.”
And now, O Lord, do with me according to your will, and order my spirit to be received in peace. For it is more expedient for me to die, than to live.”
وَالآنَ يَا رَبِّ، بِحَسَبِ مَشِيئَتِكَ اصْنَعْ بِي وَمُرْ أَنْ تُقْبَضَ رُوحِي بِسَلاَمٍ لأَنَّ الْمَوْتَ لِي خَيْرٌ مِنَ الْحَيَاةِ».
And so, on the same day, it happened that Sarah, the daughter of Raguel, in Rages, a city of the Medes, also heard a reproach from one of her father’s servant maids.
And so, on the same day, it happened that Sarah, the daughter of Raguel, in Rages, a city of the Medes, also heard a reproach from one of her father’s servant maids.
وَاتَّفَقَ فِي ذلِكَ الْيَوْمِ عَيْنِهِ أَنَّ سَارَةَ بْنَةَ رَعُوئِيلَ فِي رَاجِيسَ مَدِينَةِ الْمَادِيِّينَ سَمِعَتْ هِيَ أَيْضاً تَعْيِيراً مِنْ إِحْدَى جَوَارِي أَبِيهَا،
For she had been given to seven husbands, and a demon named Asmodeus had killed them, as soon as they had approached her.
For she had been given to seven husbands, and a demon named Asmodeus had killed them, as soon as they had approached her.
لأَنَّهُ كَانَ قَدْ عُقَدِ لَهَا عَلَى سَبْعَةِ رِجَالٍ، وَكَانَ شَيْطَانٌ اسْمُهُ أَزْمُودَاوُسُ يَقْتُلُهُمْ عَلَى أَثَرِ دُخُولِهِمْ عَلَيْهَا فِي الْحَالِ.
Therefore, when she corrected the maid for her fault, she answered her, saying, “May we never see son or daughter from you upon the earth, you murderess of your husbands.
Therefore, when she corrected the maid for her fault, she answered her, saying, “May we never see son or daughter from you upon the earth, you murderess of your husbands.
وَإِذْ كَانَتْ تَنْتَهِرُ الْجَارِيَةَ لِذَنْبٍ أَجَابَتْهَا قَائِلَةً: «لاَ رَأَيْنَا لَكِ ابْنَا وَلاَ ابْنَةً عَلَى الأَرْضِ، يَا قَاتِلَةَ أَزْوَاجِهَا.
Would you also kill me, just as you have already killed seven husbands?” At these words, she proceeded to an upper room of her house. And for three days and three nights, she did not eat or drink.
Would you also kill me, just as you have already killed seven husbands?” At these words, she proceeded to an upper room of her house. And for three days and three nights, she did not eat or drink.

أَتُرِيدِينَ أَنْ تَقْتُلِينِي كَمَا قَتَلْتِ سَبْعَةَ رِجَالٍ». فَلَمَّا سَمِعَتْ هذَا الْكَلاَمَ صَعِدَتْ إِلَى عُلِّيَّةِ بَيْتِهَا، فَأَقَامَتْ ثَلاَثَةَ أَيَّامٍ وَثَلاَثَ لَيَالٍ لاَ تَأْكُلُ وَلاَ تَشْرَبُ،
But, continuing in prayer with tears, she beseeched God, so that he would liberate her from this reproach.
But, continuing in prayer with tears, she beseeched God, so that he would liberate her from this reproach.
بَلِ اسْتَمَرَّتْ تُصَلِّي وَتَتَضَرَّعُ إِلَى اللهِ بِدُمُوعٍ أَنْ يَكْشِفَ عَنْهَا هذَا الْعَارَ.
And it happened on the third day, while she was completing her prayer, blessing the Lord,
And it happened on the third day, while she was completing her prayer, blessing the Lord,
وَلَمَّا أَتَمَّتْ صَلاَتَهَا فِي الْيَوْمِ الثَّالِثِ وَبَارَكَتِ الرَّبَّ،
that she said: “Blessed is your name, O God of our fathers, who, though you had been angry, will show mercy. And in time of tribulation, you dismiss the sins of those who call upon you.
that she said: “Blessed is your name, O God of our fathers, who, though you had been angry, will show mercy. And in time of tribulation, you dismiss the sins of those who call upon you.
قَالَت: «تَبَارَكَ اسْمُكَ يَا إِلهَ آبَائِنَا الَّذِي بَعْدَ غَضَبِهِ يَصْنَعُ الرَّحْمَةَ، وَفِي زَمَانِ الْبُؤْسِ يَغْفِرُ الْخَطَايَا لِلَّذِينَ يَدْعُونَهُ.
To you, O Lord, I turn my face; to you, I direct my eyes.
To you, O Lord, I turn my face; to you, I direct my eyes.
إِلَيْكَ يَا رَبِّ أُقْبِلُ بِوَجْهِي، وَإِلَيْكَ أَصْرِفُ نَاظِرَيَّ.
I beg you, O Lord, that you may absolve me from the chains of this reproach, or at least take me away from the earth.
I beg you, O Lord, that you may absolve me from the chains of this reproach, or at least take me away from the earth.
أَتَوَسَّلُ إِلَيْكَ يَا رَبِّ أَنْ تَحُلَّنِي مِنْ وِثَاقِ هذَا الْعَارِ أَوْ تَأْخُذَنِي عَنِ الأَرْضِ.
You know, O Lord, that I have never coveted a husband, and I have preserved my soul clean from all impure desire.
You know, O Lord, that I have never coveted a husband, and I have preserved my soul clean from all impure desire.
أَنَّك يَا رَبِّ عَالِمٌ بَأَنِي لَمْ أَشْتَهِ رَجُلاً قَطُّ، وَأَنِّي قَدْ صُنْتُ نَفْسِي مُنَزَّهَةً عَنْ كُلِّ شَهْوَةٍ،
I have never mingled myself with those who play. And I have not presented myself as a participant with those who walk with levity.
I have never mingled myself with those who play. And I have not presented myself as a participant with those who walk with levity.
وَلَمْ أَكُنْ قَطُّ أُمَازِجُ أَرْبَابَ الْمَلاَهِي، وَلاَ أُعَاشِرُ السَّالِكِينَ بِالطَّيْشِ،
But I consented to accept a husband, in your fear, not in my lust.
But I consented to accept a husband, in your fear, not in my lust.
وَإِنَّمَا رَضِيتُ بِأَنْ أَتَّخِذَ رَجُلاً لِخَوْفِكَ لاَ لِشَهْوَتِي.
And, either I was unworthy of them, or they perhaps were not worthy of me. For perhaps you have preserved me for another husband.
And, either I was unworthy of them, or they perhaps were not worthy of me. For perhaps you have preserved me for another husband.
وَلَعَلِّي لَمْ أَكُنْ مُسْتَأْهِلَةً لَهُمْ أَوْ لَمْ يَكُونُوا مُسْتَحِقِّينَ لِي، فَلَعَلَّكَ أَبْقَيْتَنِي لِبَعْلٍ آخَرَ،
For your counsel is not within the ability of man.
For your counsel is not within the ability of man.
لأَنَّ مَشُورَتَكَ لاَ يُدْرِكُهَا إِنْسَانٌ.

But all who worship you are certain of this: that one’s life, if it should be tested, shall be crowned, and if it should be in tribulation, shall be delivered, and if it should be corrected, shall be permitted to approach your mercy.

But all who worship you are certain of this: that one’s life, if it should be tested, shall be crowned, and if it should be in tribulation, shall be delivered, and if it should be corrected, shall be permitted to approach your mercy.

عَلَى أَنَّ مَنْ يَعْبُدُكَ يُوقِنُ أَنَّ حَيَاتَهُ إِنِ انْقَضَتْ بِالْمِحَنِ فَسَتَفُوزُ بِإِكْلِيلِهَا، وَإِنْ حَلَّتْ بِهِ شِدَّةٌ فَسَيُنْقَذُ، وَإِنْ عُرِضَ عَلَى التَّأْدِيبِ فَلَهُ أَنْ يَرْجِعَ إِلَى رحمتك
For you are not delighted with our perdition. For, after a storm, you create tranquility, and after tears and weeping, you pour out exultation.
For you are not delighted with our perdition. For, after a storm, you create tranquility, and after tears and weeping, you pour out exultation.
لأَنَّكَ لاَ تُسَرُّ بِهَلاَكِنَا، فَتُلْقِي السَّكِينَةَ بَعْدَ الْعَاصِفَةِ، وَبَعْدَ الْبُكَاءِ وَالنَّحِيبِ تُفِيضُ التَّهَلُّلَ.
May your name, O God of Israel, be blessed forever.”
May your name, O God of Israel, be blessed forever.”
فَلْيَكُنِ اسْمُكَ يَا إِلهَ إِسْرَائِيلَ مُبَارَكاً مَدَى الدُّهُورِ».
At that time, the prayers of them both were heard in the sight of the glory of the most high God.
At that time, the prayers of them both were heard in the sight of the glory of the most high God.
فِي ذلِكَ الْحِينِ اسْتُجِيبَتْ صَلَوَاتُ الاِثْنَيْنِ أَمَامَ مَجْدِ اللهِ الْعَلِيِّ،
And the holy Angel of the Lord, Raphael, was sent to care for both of them, whose prayers were recited at the same time in the sight of the Lord.
And the holy Angel of the Lord, Raphael, was sent to care for both of them, whose prayers were recited at the same time in the sight of the Lord.
فَأَرْسَلَ الرَّبُّ مَلاَكَهُ الْقِدِّيسَ رَافَائِيلَ لِيَشْفِيَ كِلاَ الاِثْنَيْنِ اللَّذَيْنَ رُفِعَتْ صَلَوَاتُهُمَا فِي وَقْتٍ وَاحِدٍ إِلَى حَضْرَةِ الرَّبِّ.
Therefore, when Tobit considered that his prayer was heard, so that he might be able to die, he called his son Tobias to him.
Therefore, when Tobit considered that his prayer was heard, so that he might be able to die, he called his son Tobias to him.
وَإِذْ خَالَ طُوبِيَّا أَنْ قَدِ اسْتُجِيبَتْ صَلاَتُهُ، وَتَهَيَّأَ لَهُ أَنْ يَمُوتَ، اسْتَدْعَى إِلَيْهِ طُوبِيَّا ابْنَهُ.
And he said to him: “My son, hear the words of my mouth, and set them, like a foundation, in your heart.
And he said to him: “My son, hear the words of my mouth, and set them, like a foundation, in your heart.
وَقَالَ لَهُ: «اسْمَعْ يَا بُنَيَّ كَلِمَاتِ فِيَّ، وَاجْعَلْهَا فِي قَلْبِكَ مِثْلَ الأَسَاسِ.
When God will receive my soul, bury my body. And you shall honor your mother, all the days of her life.
When God will receive my soul, bury my body. And you shall honor your mother, all the days of her life.
إِذَا قَبَضَ اللهُ نَفْسِي، فَادْفِنْ جَسَدِي، وَأَكْرِمْ وَالِدَتَكَ جَمِيعَ أَيَّامِ حَيَاتِهَا،
For you are obliged to be mindful of what great perils she suffered because of you in her womb.
For you are obliged to be mindful of what great perils she suffered because of you in her womb.
وَاذْكُرْ مَا الْمَشَقَّاتُ الَّتِي عَانَتْهَا لأَجْلِكَ فِي جَوْفِهَا وَمَا كَانَ أَشَدَّهَا.
But when she too will have completed the time of her life, bury her near me.
But when she too will have completed the time of her life, bury her near me.
وَمَتَى اسْتَوْفَتْ هِيَ أَيْضاً زَمَانَ حَيَاتِهَا، فَادْفِنْهَا إِلَى جَانِبِي.
Yet, for all the days of your life, have God in your mind. And be careful that you never consent to sin, nor overlook the precepts of the Lord our God.
Yet, for all the days of your life, have God in your mind. And be careful that you never consent to sin, nor overlook the precepts of the Lord our God.
وَأَنْتَ فَلْيَكُنِ اللهُ فِي قَلْبِكَ جَمِيعَ أَيَّامِ حَيَاتِكَ، وَاحْذَرْ أَنْ تَرْضَى بِالْخَطِيئَةِ وَتَتَعَدَّى وَصَايَا الرَّبِّ إِلهِنَا.
Give alms from your substance, and do not turn away your face from any pauper. For so it shall be that neither will the face of the Lord be turned away from you.
Give alms from your substance, and do not turn away your face from any pauper. For so it shall be that neither will the face of the Lord be turned away from you.
تَصَدَّقَ مِنْ مَالِكَ وَلاَ تُحَوِّلْ وَجْهَكَ عَنْ فَقِيرٍ، وَحِينَئِذٍ فَوَجْهُ الرَّبِّ لاَ يُحَوَّلُ عَنْكَ.
In whatever way that you are able, so shall you be merciful.
In whatever way that you are able, so shall you be merciful.
كُنْ رَحِيماً عَلَى قَدْرِ طَاقَتِكَ.
If you have much, distribute abundantly. If you have little, nevertheless strive to bestow a little freely.
If you have much, distribute abundantly. If you have little, nevertheless strive to bestow a little freely.
إِنْ كَانَ لَكَ كَثِيرٌ، فَابْذُلْ كَثِيراً؛ وَإِنْ كَانَ لَكَ قَلِيلٌ، فَاجْتَهِدْ أَنْ تَبْذُلَ الْقَلِيلَ عَنْ نَفْسٍ طَيِّبَةٍ.
For you store up for yourself a good reward for the day of necessity.
For you store up for yourself a good reward for the day of necessity.
فَإِنَّكَ تَدَّخِرُ لَكَ ثَوَاباً جَمِيلاً إِلَى يَوْمِ الضَّرُورَةِ،
For almsgiving liberates from every sin and from death, and it will not suffer the soul to go into darkness.
For almsgiving liberates from every sin and from death, and it will not suffer the soul to go into darkness.
لأَنَّ الصَّدَقَةَ تُنَجِّي مِنْ كُلِّ خَطِيئَةٍ وَمِنَ الْمَوْتِ، وَلاَ تَدَعُ النَّفْسَ تَصِيرُ إِلَى الظُّلْمَةِ.
Almsgiving will be a great act of faith before the most high God, for all those who practice it.
Almsgiving will be a great act of faith before the most high God, for all those who practice it.
إِنَّ الصَّدَقَةَ هِيَ رَجَاءٌ عَظِيمٌ عِنْدَ اللهِ الْعَلِيِّ لِجَمِيعِ صَانِعِيهَا.
Take care to keep yourself, my son, from all fornication, and, except for your wife, never permit yourself to know such an offense.
Take care to keep yourself, my son, from all fornication, and, except for your wife, never permit yourself to know such an offense.
اِحْذَرْ لِنَفْسِكَ يَا بُنَيَّ مِنْ كُلِّ زِنًى، وَلاَ تَتَجَاوَزِ امْرَأَتَكَ مُسْتَبِيحاً مَعْرِفَةَ الإِثْمِ أَبَداً.
Never permit arrogance to rule in your mind or in your words. For in it, all perdition had its beginning.
Never permit arrogance to rule in your mind or in your words. For in it, all perdition had its beginning.
وَلاَ تَدَعِ الْكِبْرَ يَسْتَوْلِي عَلَى أَفْكَارِكَ أَوْ أَقْوَالِكَ، لأَنَّ الْكِبْرَ مَبْدَأُ كُلِّ هلاك
And whoever has done any kind of work for you, immediately pay him his wages, and do not let the wages of your hired hand remain with you at all.
And whoever has done any kind of work for you, immediately pay him his wages, and do not let the wages of your hired hand remain with you at all.
وَكُلُّ مَنْ خَدَمَكَ بِشَيْءٍ فَأَوْفِهِ أُجْرَتَهُ لِسَاعَتِهِ، وَأُجْرَةُ أَجِيرِكَ لاَ تَبْقَ عِنْدَكَ أَبَداً.
Whatever you would hate to have done to you by another, see that you never do so to another.
Whatever you would hate to have done to you by another, see that you never do so to another.
كُلُّ مَا تَكْرَهُ أَنْ يَفْعَلَهُ غَيْرُكَ بِكَ فَإِيَّاكَ أَنْ تَفْعَلَهُ أَنْتَ بِغَيْرِكَ.
Eat your bread with the hungry and the needy, and cover the naked with your own garments.
Eat your bread with the hungry and the needy, and cover the naked with your own garments.
كُلْ خُبْزَكَ مَعَ الْجِيَاعِ وَالْمَسَاكِينِ، وَاكْسُ الْعُرَاةَ مِنْ ثِيَابِكَ.
Set out your bread and your wine at the burial of a just man, and do not eat and drink from it with sinners.
Set out your bread and your wine at the burial of a just man, and do not eat and drink from it with sinners.
ضَعْ خُبْزَكَ وَخَمْرَكَ عَلَى مَدْفِنِ الْبَارِّ، وَلاَ تَأْكُلْ وَلاَ تَشْرَبْ مِنْهُمَا مَعَ الْخَطَأَةِ.
Always seek the counsel of a wise man.
Always seek the counsel of a wise man.
اِلْتَمِسْ مَشُورَةَ الْحَكِيمِ دَائِماً.
Bless God at all times. And petition him that he direct your ways and that all your counsels may remain in him.
Bless God at all times. And petition him that he direct your ways and that all your counsels may remain in him.
وَبَارِكِ اللهَ فِي كُلِّ حِينٍ وَاسْتَرْشِدْهُ لِتَقْوِيمِ سُبُلِكَ وَإِقْرَارِ كُلِّ مَشُورَاتِكِ فِيهِ.
And now, I reveal to you, my son, that I lent ten talents of silver, while you were still a young child, to Gabael, in Rages, a city of the Medes, and I have his written agreement with me.
And now, I reveal to you, my son, that I lent ten talents of silver, while you were still a young child, to Gabael, in Rages, a city of the Medes, and I have his written agreement with me.
ثُمَّ اعْلَمْ يَا بُنَيَّ، أَنِّي قَدْ أَعْطَيْتُ، وَأَنْتَ صَغِيرٌ، عَشَرَةَ قَنَاطِيرَ مِنَ الْفِضَّةِ لِغَابِيلُوسَ فِي رَاجِيسَ مَدِينَةِ الْمَادِيِّينَ، وَمَعِي بِهَا صَكٌّ.
And so, inquire how you may travel to him and receive from him the aforementioned weight of silver, and return to him the written agreement.
And so, inquire how you may travel to him and receive from him the aforementioned weight of silver, and return to him the written agreement.
وَحَيْثُ ذلِكَ، فَانْظُرْ كَيْفَ تَتَوَصَّلُ إِلَيْهِ، فَتَقْبِضُ مِنْهُ الزِّنَةَ الْمَذْكُورَةَ مِنَ الْفِضَّةِ، وَتَرُدُّ عَلَيْهِ صَكَّهُ.
Do not be afraid, my son. We do indeed lead a poor life, but we will have many good things: if we fear God, and withdraw from all sin, and do what is good.”
Do not be afraid, my son. We do indeed lead a poor life, but we will have many good things: if we fear God, and withdraw from all sin, and do what is good.”
وَلاَ تَخَفْ يَا وَلَدَي، فَإِنَّا نَعِيشُ عِيشَةَ الْفُقَرَاءِ، وَلكِنْ سَيَكُونُ لَنَا خَيْرٌ كَثِيرٌ إِذَا اتَّقَيْنَا اللهَ وَابْتَعَدْنَا عَنْ كُلِّ خَطِيئَةٍ وَفَعَلْنَا خَيْراً».
Then Tobias responded to his father, and he said: “I will do everything just as you have instructed me, father.
Then Tobias responded to his father, and he said: “I will do everything just as you have instructed me, father.
فَأَجَابَ طُوبِيَّا أَبَاهُ وَقَالَ: «يَا أَبَتِ كُلُّ مَّا أَمَرْتَنِي بِهِ أَفْعَلُهُ.
But I do not know how to obtain this money. He does not know me, and I do not know him. What proof shall I give to him? And I do not know any part of the way, which leads to that place.”
But I do not know how to obtain this money. He does not know me, and I do not know him. What proof shall I give to him? And I do not know any part of the way, which leads to that place.”

وَأَمَّا هذَا الْمَالُ فَمَا أَدْرِي كَيْفَ أُحَصِّلُهُ، فَإِنَّ الرَّجُلَ لاَ يَعْرِفُنِي، وَأَنَا لاَ أَعْرِفُهُ؛ فَمَا الْعَلاَمَةُ الَّتِي أُعْطِيهَا لَهُ؟ بَلِ الطَّرِيقُ الَّتِي تُؤَدِّي إِلَى هُنَاكَ لاَ أَعْرِفُهَا أَيْضاً».
Then his father answered him, and he said: “Indeed, I have a written agreement about that in my possession, which, when you show it to him, he will immediately repay it.
Then his father answered him, and he said: “Indeed, I have a written agreement about that in my possession, which, when you show it to him, he will immediately repay it.
فَأَجَابَهُ أَبُوهُ وَقَالَ: «إِنَّ عِنْدِي صَكَّهُ، فَإِذَا عَرَضْتَهُ عَلَيْهِ فَإِنَّهُ يُؤَدِّي عَاجِلاً.
But go out now, and inquire after some faithful man, who would go with you to keep you safe in return for his wages, so that you may receive it while I am still alive.”
But go out now, and inquire after some faithful man, who would go with you to keep you safe in return for his wages, so that you may receive it while I am still alive.”
وَالآنَ هَلُمَّ فَالْتَمِسْ لَكَ رَجُلاً ثِقَةً يَصْحَبُكَ بِأُجْرَتِهِ حَتَّى تَسْتَوْفِيَ الْمَالَ وَأَنَا حَيٌّ».
Then Tobias, departing, found a splendid young man, standing girded and seemingly ready for travel.
Then Tobias, departing, found a splendid young man, standing girded and seemingly ready for travel.
فَبَيْنَمَا خَرَجَ طُوبِيَّا، إِذَا بِفَتًى بَهِيٍّ قَدْ وَقَفَ مُشَمِّراً كَأَنَّهُ مُتَأَهِّبٌ لِلْمَسِيرِ.
And not knowing that he was an Angel of God, he greeted him, and he said, “Where are you from, good young man?”
And not knowing that he was an Angel of God, he greeted him, and he said, “Where are you from, good young man?”
فَسَلَّمَ عَلَيْهِ وَهُوَ يَجْهَلُ أَنَّهُ مَلاَكُ اللهِ وَقَالَ: «مِنْ أَيْنَ أَقْبَلْتَ يَا فَتَى الْخَيْرِ؟»
And so he answered, “From the sons of Israel.” And Tobias said to him, “Do you know the way that leads to the region of the Medes?”
And so he answered, “From the sons of Israel.” And Tobias said to him, “Do you know the way that leads to the region of the Medes?”
قَالَ: «أَنَا مِنْ بَنِي إِسْرَائِيلَ». فَقَالَ لَهُ طُوبِيَّا: «هَلْ تَعْرِفُ الطَّرِيقَ الآخِذَةَ إِلَى بِلاَدِ الْمَادِيِّينَ؟»

And he answered: “I know it. And I have frequently walked through all its paths, and I stayed with Gabael, our brother, who lives in Rages, a city of the Medes, which is situated at the mount of Ecbatana.”

And he answered: “I know it. And I have frequently walked through all its paths, and I stayed with Gabael, our brother, who lives in Rages, a city of the Medes, which is situated at the mount of Ecbatana.”
قَالَ: «أَعْرِفُهَا، وَقَدْ سَلَكْتُ جَمِيعَ طُرُقِهَا مِرَاراً كَثِيرَةً، وَكُنْتُ نَازِلاً بِأَخِينَا غَابِيلُوسَ الْمُقِيمِ بِرَاجِيسِ مَدِينَةِ الْمَادِيِّينَ الَّتِي فِي جَبَلِ أَحْمَتَا».
Tobias said to him, “I implore you, wait here for me, until I tell these same things to my father.”
Tobias said to him, “I implore you, wait here for me, until I tell these same things to my father.”
فَقَالَ لَهُ طُوبِيَّا: «انْتَظِرْنِي حَتَّى أُخْبِرَ أَبِي بِهذَا».
Then Tobias, entering, revealed all these things to his father. Upon which, his father, in admiration, asked that he would enter to him.
Then Tobias, entering, revealed all these things to his father. Upon which, his father, in admiration, asked that he would enter to him.
وَدَخَلَ طُوبِيَّا وَأَخْبَرَ أَبَاهُ بِجَمِيعِ ذلِكَ، فَتَعَجَّبَ أَبُوهُ، وَطَلَبَ أَنْ يَدْخُلَ عَلَيْهِ.
And so, entering, he greeted him, and he said, “May gladness be always with you.”
And so, entering, he greeted him, and he said, “May gladness be always with you.”
فَدَخَلَ وَسَلَّمَ عَلَيْهِ وَقَالَ: «لِيَكُنْ لَكَ فَرَحٌ دَائِمٌ».
And Tobit said, “What kind of gladness will be for me, since I sit in darkness and do not see the light of heaven?”
And Tobit said, “What kind of gladness will be for me, since I sit in darkness and do not see the light of heaven?”
فَأَجَابَ طُوبِيَّا: «وَأَيُّ فَرَحٍ يَكُونُ لِي أَنَا الْمُقِيمَ فِي الظَّلاَمِ لاَ أُبْصِرُ ضَوْءَ السَّمَاءِ؟»
And the young man said to him, “Be steadfast in soul. Your cure from God is near.”
And the young man said to him, “Be steadfast in soul. Your cure from God is near.”
فَقَالَ لَهُ الْفَتَى: «كُنْ طَيِّبَ الْقَلْبِ فَإِنَّكَ عَنْ قَلِيلٍ تَنَالُ الْبُرْءَ مِنْ لَدُنِ اللهِ».
And so Tobit said to him, “Are you able to lead my son to Gabael in Rages, a city of the Medes? And when you return, I will pay you your wages.”
And so Tobit said to him, “Are you able to lead my son to Gabael in Rages, a city of the Medes? And when you return, I will pay you your wages.”
فَقَالَ لَهُ طُوبِيَّا: «هَلْ لَكَ أَنْ تُبَلِّغَ ابْنِي إِلَى غَابِيلُوسَ فِي رَاجِيسَ مَدِينَةِ الْمَادِيِّينَ، وَأَنَا أُوفِيكَ أُجْرَتَكَ مَتَى رَجَعْتَ؟»
And the Angel said to him, “I will lead him, and I will bring him back to you.”
And the Angel said to him, “I will lead him, and I will bring him back to you.”
فَقَالَ لَهُ المَلاَكُ: «آخُذُهُ وَأَعُودُ بِهِ إِلَيْكَ».
And Tobit responded to him, “I ask you to tell me: which family or which tribe are you from?”
And Tobit responded to him, “I ask you to tell me: which family or which tribe are you from?”
فَقَالَ لَهُ طُوبِيَّا: «أَخْبِرْنِي مِنْ أَيِّ عَشِيرَةٍ وَمِنْ أَيِّ سِبْطٍ أَنْتَ؟»
And Raphael the Angel said, “Do you seek the family of the one you hire, or else the hired hand himself, to go with your son?
And Raphael the Angel said, “Do you seek the family of the one you hire, or else the hired hand himself, to go with your son?
فَقَالَ لَهُ رَافَائِيلُ الْمَلاَكُ: «أَفِي نَسَبِ الأَجِيرِ حَاجَتُكَ أَمْ فِي الأَجِيرِ الَّذِي يَذْهَبُ مَعَ ابْنِكَ؟
But, lest perhaps I cause you to worry: I am Azariah, the son of Hananiah the great.”
But, lest perhaps I cause you to worry: I am Azariah, the son of Hananiah the great.”
وَلكِنْ لِكَيْ لاَ أُقْلِقَ بَالَكَ، أَنَا عَزَرْيَا بْنُ حَنَنْيَا الْعَظِيمِ».
And Tobit responded, “You are from a great family. But I ask you, do not be angry that I wanted to know your family.”
And Tobit responded, “You are from a great family. But I ask you, do not be angry that I wanted to know your family.”
فَقَالَ لَهُ طُوبِيَّا: «إِنَّكَ مِنْ نَسَبٍ كَرِيمٍ غَيْرَ أَنِّي أَرْجُو أَنْ لاَ يَسُوءَكَ كَوْنِي طَلَبْتُ مَعْرِفَةَ نَسَبِكَ».
But the Angel said to him, “I will lead your son safely, and I will bring him back safely to you.”
But the Angel said to him, “I will lead your son safely, and I will bring him back safely to you.”
فَقَالَ لَهُ الْمَلاَكُ: «هَأَنَذَا آخُذَ ابْنَكَ سَالِماً، وَسَأَعُودُ بِهِ إِلَيْكَ سَالِماً».
And so Tobit, answering, said, “May you walk well, and may God be with you on your journey, and may his Angel accompany you.”
And so Tobit, answering, said, “May you walk well, and may God be with you on your journey, and may his Angel accompany you.”
قَالَ طُوبِيَّا: «انْطَلِقَا بِسَلاَمٍ، وَلِيَكُنِ اللهُ فِي طَرِيقِكُمَا وَمَلاَكُهُ يُرَافِقُكُمَا».
Then, when all things were ready which were to be carried on their journey, Tobias said farewell to his father, and to his mother, and the two of them walked together.
Then, when all things were ready which were to be carried on their journey, Tobias said farewell to his father, and to his mother, and the two of them walked together.
حِينَئِذٍ أَخَذَا كُلَّ مَا أَرَادَا أَخْذَهُ مِنْ أُهْبَةِ الطَّرِيقِ، وَوَدَّعَ طُوبِيَّا أَبَاهُ وَأُمَّهُ، وَسَارَا كِلاَهُمَا مَعاً.
And when they had set out, his mother began to weep, and to say: “You have taken the staff of our old age, and you have sent him away from us.
And when they had set out, his mother began to weep, and to say: “You have taken the staff of our old age, and you have sent him away from us.
فَلَمَّا فَصَلاَ، جَعَلَتْ أُمُّهُ تَبْكِي وَتَقُولُ: «قَدْ أَخَذْتَ عُكَّازَةَ شَيْخُوخَتِنَا وَأَبْعَدْتَهَا عَنَّا.
I wish that the money, for which you sent him, had never been.
I wish that the money, for which you sent him, had never been.
لاَ كَانَ هذَا الْمَالُ الَّذِي أَرْسَلْتَهُ لأَجْلِهِ.
For our poverty has been sufficient for us, so that we might count it as riches that we saw our son.”
For our poverty has been sufficient for us, so that we might count it as riches that we saw our son.”
لَقَدْ كَانَ فِي رِزْقِنَا الْقَلِيلِ مَا يَكْفِي لأَنْ نَعُدَّ النَّظَرَ إِلَى وَلَدِنَا غِنًى عَظِيماً».
And Tobit said to her: “Do not weep. Our son will arrive safely, and he will return safely to us, and your eyes shall see him.
And Tobit said to her: “Do not weep. Our son will arrive safely, and he will return safely to us, and your eyes shall see him.
فَقَالَ لَهَا طُوبِيَّا: «لاَ تَبْكِي، إِنَّ وَلَدَنَا سَيَصِلُ سَالِماً وَيَعُودُ إِلَيْنَا سَالِماً، وَعَيْنَاكِ تُبْصِرَانِهِ.
For I believe that the good Angel of God accompanies him and that he orders all things well which occur around him, such that he will be returned to us with gladness.”
For I believe that the good Angel of God accompanies him and that he orders all things well which occur around him, such that he will be returned to us with gladness.”
فَإِنِّي وَاثِقٌ بِأَنَّ مَلاَكَ اللهِ الصَّالِحَ يَصْحَبُهُ وَيُدَبِّرُهُ فِي جَمِيعِ أَحْوَالِهِ حَتَّى يَرْجِعَ إِلَيْنَا بِفَرَحٍ».
At these words, his mother ceased weeping, and she was silent.
At these words, his mother ceased weeping, and she was silent.
فَكَفَّتْ أُمُّهُ عَنِ الْبُكَاءِ عِنْدَ هذَا الْكَلاَمِ وَسَكَتَتْ.
And so Tobias continued on, and the dog was following him, and he stayed at the first stopping point, near the river Tigris.
And so Tobias continued on, and the dog was following him, and he stayed at the first stopping point, near the river Tigris.
وَسَافَرَ طُوبِيَّا وَالْكَلْبُ يَتْبَعُهُ، فَبَاتَ أَوَّلَ مَنْزِلَةٍ بِجَانِبِ نَهْرِ دِجْلَةَ.
And he went out to wash his feet, and behold, an immense fish came out to devour him.
And he went out to wash his feet, and behold, an immense fish came out to devour him.
وَخَرَجَ لِيَغْسِلَ رِجْلَيْهِ، فَإِذَا بِحُوتٍ عَظِيمٍ قَدْ خَرَجَ لِيَفْتَرِسَهُ.
And Tobias, being frightened of it, cried out with a loud voice, saying, “Sir, it is attacking me!”
And Tobias, being frightened of it, cried out with a loud voice, saying, “Sir, it is attacking me!”
فَارْتَاعَ طُوبِيَّا وَصَرَخَ بِصَوْتٍ عَظِيمٍ قَائِلاً: «يَا مَوْلاَيَ قَدِ اقْتَحَمَنِي».
And the Angel said to him, “Catch it by the gills, and draw it to you.” And when he had done so, he pulled it onto dry land, and it began to thrash before his feet.
And the Angel said to him, “Catch it by the gills, and draw it to you.” And when he had done so, he pulled it onto dry land, and it began to thrash before his feet.
فَقَالَ لَهُ الْمَلاَكُ: «أَمْسِكْ بِخَيْشُومِهِ وَاجْتَذِبْهُ إِلَيْكَ». فَفَعَلَ كَذلِكَ وَاجْتَذَبَهُ إِلَى الْيَبْسِ، فَأَخَذَ يَخْتَبِطُ عِنْدَ رِجْلَيْهِ.
Then the Angel said to him: “Disembowel this fish, and put aside his heart, and his gall, and his liver for yourself. For these things are necessary as useful medicines.”
Then the Angel said to him: “Disembowel this fish, and put aside his heart, and his gall, and his liver for yourself. For these things are necessary as useful medicines.”
فَقَالَ لَهُ الْمَلاَكُ: «شُقَّ جَوْفَ الْحُوتِ، وَاحْتَفِظْ بِقَلْبِهِ وَمَرَارَتِهِ وَكَبِدِهِ، فَإِنَّ لَكَ بِهَا مَنْفَعَةً لِعِلاَجٍ مُفِيدٍ».

And when he had done so, he roasted its flesh, and they took it with them on the way. The rest they salted, so that it might be sufficient for them, until they would arrive at Rages, a city of the Medes.

And when he had done so, he roasted its flesh, and they took it with them on the way. The rest they salted, so that it might be sufficient for them, until they would arrive at Rages, a city of the Medes.
فَفَعَلَ كَذلِكَ، ثُمَّ شَوَى مِنْ لَحْمِهِ، فَأَخَذَا لِلطَّرِيقِ، وَمَلَّحَا سَائِرَهُ حَتَّى يَكُونَ لَهُمَا مَا يَكْفِيهِمَا إِلَى أَنْ يَبْلُغَا رَاجِيسَ مَدِينَةَ الْمَادِيِّينَ.
Then Tobias questioned the Angel, and he said to him, “I implore you, brother Azariah, to tell me what remedies these things hold, which you have told me to retain from the fish?”
Then Tobias questioned the Angel, and he said to him, “I implore you, brother Azariah, to tell me what remedies these things hold, which you have told me to retain from the fish?”

ثُمَّ إِنَّ طُوبِيَّا سَأَلَ الْمَلاَكَ وَقَالَ لَهُ: «نَشَدْتُكَ يَا أَخِي عَزَرْيَا أَنْ تُخْبِرَنِي مَا الْعِلاَجُ الَّذِي يُؤْخَذُ مِنْ هذِهِ الأَشْيَاءِ الَّتِي أَمَرْتَنِي أَنْ أَذْخَرَهَا مِنَ الْحُوتِ؟»

And the Angel, answering, said to him: “If you put a little piece of its heart on burning coals, its smoke will drive away all kinds of demons, whether from a man or from a woman, so that they will no longer approach them.

And the Angel, answering, said to him: “If you put a little piece of its heart on burning coals, its smoke will drive away all kinds of demons, whether from a man or from a woman, so that they will no longer approach them.

فَأَجَابَهُ الْمَلاَكُ قَائِلاً: «إِذَا أَلْقَيْتَ شَيْئاً مِنْ قَلْبِهِ عَلَى الْجَمْرِ فَدُخَانُهُ يَطْرُدُ كُلَّ جِنْسٍ مِنَ الشَيَّاطِينِ فِي رَجُلٍ كَانَ أَوِ امْرَأَةٍ بِحَيْثُ لاَ يَعُودُ يَقْرُبُهُمَا أَبَداً.
And the gall is useful for anointing the eyes, in which there may be a white speck, and they will be cured.”
And the gall is useful for anointing the eyes, in which there may be a white speck, and they will be cured.”
وَالْمَرَارَةُ تَنْفَعُ لِمَسْحِ الْعُيُونِ الَّتِي عَلَيْهَا غِشَاءٌ فَتَبْرَأُ».
And Tobias said to him, “Where do you prefer that we stay?”
And Tobias said to him, “Where do you prefer that we stay?”
وَقَالَ طُوبِيَّا: «أَيْنَ تُرِيدُ أَنْ نَنْزِلَ؟»
And the Angel, responding, said: “Here is one named Raguel, a man closely related to you from your tribe, and he has a daughter named Sarah, but he has no other male or female, except her.
And the Angel, responding, said: “Here is one named Raguel, a man closely related to you from your tribe, and he has a daughter named Sarah, but he has no other male or female, except her.
فَقَالَ الْمَلاَكُ إِنَّ هُنَا رَجُلاً اسْمُهُ رَعُوئِيلُ مِنْ ذَوِي قَرَابَتِكَ مِنْ سِبْطِكَ، وَلَهُ بِنْتٌ اسْمُهَا سَارَةُ، وَلَيْسَ لَهُ مِنْ ذَكَرٍ وَلاَ أُنْثَى سِوَاهَا.
All his livelihood is dependent upon you, and you ought to take her to yourself in marriage.
All his livelihood is dependent upon you, and you ought to take her to yourself in marriage.
فَجَمِيعُ مَالِهِ مُسْتَحَقٌّ لَكَ، وَلاَ بُدَّ لَكَ أَنْ تَتَّخِذَهَا زَوْجَةً.
Therefore, ask for her from her father, and he will give her to you as wife.”
Therefore, ask for her from her father, and he will give her to you as wife.”
فَاخْطُبْهَا إِلَى أَبِيهَا فَإِنَّهُ يُزَوِّجُهَا مِنْكَ».
Then Tobias responded, and he said: “I hear that she has been given to seven husbands, and they passed away. But I have even heard this: that a demon killed them.
Then Tobias responded, and he said: “I hear that she has been given to seven husbands, and they passed away. But I have even heard this: that a demon killed them.
فَأَجَابَ طُوبِيَّا وَقَالَ: «إِنِّي سَمِعْتُ أَنَّهُ قَدْ عُقِدَ لَهَا عَلَى سَبْعَةِ أَزْوَاجٍ فَمَاتُوا، وَقَدْ سَمِعْتُ أَيْضاً أَنَّ الشَّيْطَانَ قَتَلَهُمْ.
Therefore, I am afraid, lest this may happen to me also. And since I am the only child of my parents, I might send their old age with sorrow to the grave.”
Therefore, I am afraid, lest this may happen to me also. And since I am the only child of my parents, I might send their old age with sorrow to the grave.”
فَلأَجْلِ هذَا أَخَافُ أَنْ يُصِيبَنِي مِثْلُ ذلِكَ، وَأَنَا وَحِيدٌ لأَبَوَيَّ، فَأَنْزِلُ شَيْخُوخَتَهُمَا إِلَى الْجَحِيمِ بِالْحُزْنِ».
Then the Angel Raphael said to him: “Listen to me, and I will reveal to you who they are, over whom the demon can prevail.
Then the Angel Raphael said to him: “Listen to me, and I will reveal to you who they are, over whom the demon can prevail.
فَقَالَ لَهُ الْمَلاَكُ رَافَائِيلُ: «اسْتَمِعْ فَأُخْبِرَكَ مَنْ هُمُ الَّذِينَ يَسْتَطِيعُ الشَّيْطَانُ أَنْ يَقْوَى عَلَيْهِمْ؟

For example, those who receive marriage in such manner as to exclude God from themselves and from their mind, and in such a manner as to empty themselves to their lust, like the horse and mule, which have no understanding, over them the demon has power.

For example, those who receive marriage in such manner as to exclude God from themselves and from their mind, and in such a manner as to empty themselves to their lust, like the horse and mule, which have no understanding, over them the demon has power.

إِنَّ الَّذِينَ يَتَزَوَّجُونَ، فَيَنْفُونَ اللهَ مِنْ قُلُوبِهِمْ، وَيَتَفَرَّغُونَ لِشَهْوَتِهِمْ كَالْفَرَسِ وَالْبَغْلِ اللَّذَيْنِ لاَ فَهْمَ لَهُمَا؛ أُولئِكَ لِلشَّيْطَانِ عَلَيْهِمْ سُلْطَانٌ.
But you, when you will have accepted her, enter the bedroom and for three days keep yourself continent from her, and empty yourself to nothing other than prayers with her.
But you, when you will have accepted her, enter the bedroom and for three days keep yourself continent from her, and empty yourself to nothing other than prayers with her.
فَأَنْتَ إِذَا تَزَوَّجْتَهَا وَدَخَلْتَ الْمُخْدَعَ، فَأَمْسِكْ عَنْهَا ثَلاَثَةَ أَيَّامٍ وَلاَ تَتَفَرَّغْ مَعَهَا إِلاَّ لِلصَّلَوَاتِ.
Moreover, on that night, burn the liver of the fish like incense, and the demon will be put to flight.
Moreover, on that night, burn the liver of the fish like incense, and the demon will be put to flight.
وَفِي تِلْكَ اللَّيْلَةِ، إِذَا أَحْرَقْتَ كَبِدَ الْحُوتِ، يَنْهَزِمُ الشَّيْطَانُ.
In truth, on the second night, you will become ready to receive a physical union like that of the holy Patriarchs.
In truth, on the second night, you will become ready to receive a physical union like that of the holy Patriarchs.
وَفِي اللَّيْلَةِ الثَّانِيَةِ، تَكُونُ مَقْبُولاً فِي شَرِكَةِ الآبَاءِ الْقِدِّيسِينَ.
And then, on the third night, you will obtain a blessing, so that healthy children may be procreated from you both.
And then, on the third night, you will obtain a blessing, so that healthy children may be procreated from you both.
وَفِي اللَّيْلَةِ الثَّالِثَةِ، تَنَالُ الْبَرَكَةَ حَتَّى يُولَدَ لَكُمَا بَنُونَ سَالِمُونَ.

And so, the third night having been accomplished, you will receive the virgin with the fear of the Lord, led more by love of children than by physical desire, so that, as the offspring of Abraham, you will then obtain a blessing in children.

And so, the third night having been accomplished, you will receive the virgin with the fear of the Lord, led more by love of children than by physical desire, so that, as the offspring of Abraham, you will then obtain a blessing in children.
وَبَعْدَ انْقِضَاءِ اللَّيْلَةِ الثَّالِثَةِ، تَتَّخِذُ الْبِكْرَ بِخَوْفِ الرَّبِّ وَأَنْتَ رَاغِبٌ فِي الْبَنِينَ أَكْثَرَ مِنَ الشَّهْوَةِ، لِكَيْ تَنَالَ بَرَكَةَ ذُرِّيَّةِ إِبْرَاهِيمَ
And so they went to Raguel, and Raguel received them with gladness.
And so they went to Raguel, and Raguel received them with gladness.
ثُمَّ دَخَلاَ عَلَى رَعُوئِيلَ، فَتَلَقَّاهُمَا رَعُوئِيلُ بِالْمَسَرَّةِ.
And Raguel, gazing upon Tobias, said to Anna his wife, “How much like my cousin is this young man!”
And Raguel, gazing upon Tobias, said to Anna his wife, “How much like my cousin is this young man!”
وَإِذْ نَظَرَ رَعُوئِيلُ إِلَى طُوبِيَّا قَالَ لِحَنَّةَ زَوْجَتِهِ: «مَا أَشْبَهَ هذَا الرَّجُلَ بِذِي قَرَابَتِي!»
And when he had spoken this, he said, “Which of our brethren are you from, young men?”
And when he had spoken this, he said, “Which of our brethren are you from, young men?”
وَبَعْدَ هذَا الْكَلاَمِ قَالَ رَعُوئِيلُ: «مِنْ أَيْنَ أَنْتُمَا أَيُّهَا الأَخَوَانِ الْفِتْيَانِ؟» فَقَالاَ لَهُ: «مِنْ سِبْطِ نَفْتَالِيَ مِنْ جَلاَءِ نِينَوَى».
But they said, “We are from the tribe of Naphtali, from the captivity of Nineveh.”
But they said, “We are from the tribe of Naphtali, from the captivity of Nineveh.”
فَقَالَ لَهُمَا رَعُوئِيلُ: «هَلْ تَعْرِفَانِ طُوبِيَّا أَخِي؟» فَقَالاَ: «نَعْرِفُهُ».
And Raguel said to them, “Do you know my brother Tobit?” They said to him, “We know him.”
And Raguel said to them, “Do you know my brother Tobit?” They said to him, “We know him.”
فَلَمَّا أَكْثَرَ مِنَ الثَّنَاءِ عَلَيْهِ، قَالَ الْمَلاَكُ لِرَعُوئِيلَ: «إِنَّ طُوبِيَّا الَّذِي أَنْتَ تَسْأَلُ عَنْهُ هُوَ أَبُو هذَا».
And since he was saying many good things about him, the Angel said to Raguel, “The Tobit about whom you inquire is the father of this young man.”
And since he was saying many good things about him, the Angel said to Raguel, “The Tobit about whom you inquire is the father of this young man.”
فَأَلْقَى رَعُوئِيلُ بِنَفْسِهِ، وَقَبَّلَهُ بِدُمُوعٍ، وَبَكَى عَلَى عُنُقِهِ،
And Raguel threw himself towards him and kissed him with tears and weeping upon his neck, saying, “May a blessing be upon you, my son, because you are the son of a good and most noble man.”
And Raguel threw himself towards him and kissed him with tears and weeping upon his neck, saying, “May a blessing be upon you, my son, because you are the son of a good and most noble man.”
وَقَالَ: «بَرَكَةٌ لَكَ يَا بُنَيَّ، إِنَّكَ ابْنُ رَجُلٍ صَالِحٍ فَاضِلٍ».
And his wife Anna, and their daughter Sarah, were weeping.
And his wife Anna, and their daughter Sarah, were weeping.
وَبَكَتْ حَنَّةُ امْرَأَتُهُ وَسَارَةُ ابْنَتُهُمَا أَيْضاً.
And so, after they had spoken, Raguel instructed a sheep to be killed, and a feast to be prepared. And when he exhorted them to recline for dinner,
And so, after they had spoken, Raguel instructed a sheep to be killed, and a feast to be prepared. And when he exhorted them to recline for dinner,
وَبَعْدَ أَنْ تَحَادَثُوا، أَمَرَ رَعُوئِيلُ أَنْ يُذْبَحَ كَبْشٌ، وَتُهَيَّأَ مَأْدُبَةٌ وَدَعَاهُمَا أَنْ يَتَّكِئَا لِلْغَدَاءِ.
Tobias said, “Here, today, I will not eat or drink, unless you first confirm my petition, and promise to give Sarah your daughter to me.”
Tobias said, “Here, today, I will not eat or drink, unless you first confirm my petition, and promise to give Sarah your daughter to me.”
فَقَالَ طُوبِيَّا: «إِنِّي لاَ آكُلُ الْيَوْمَ طَعَاماً ههُنَا وَلاَ أَشْرَبُ مَا لَمْ تُجِبْنِي إِلَى مَا أَنَا سَائِلُهُ، وَتَعِدْنِي أَنْ تُعْطِيَنِي سَارَةَ ابْنَتَكَ».

When Raguel heard this word, he became afraid, knowing what had befallen those seven men, who had approached her. And he began to fear, lest it might happen to him also in the same way. And, since he wavered and gave no further response to the petition,

When Raguel heard this word, he became afraid, knowing what had befallen those seven men, who had approached her. And he began to fear, lest it might happen to him also in the same way. And, since he wavered and gave no further response to the petition,

فَلَمَّا سَمِعَ رَعُوئِيلُ هذَا الْكَلاَمَ، ارْتَعَدَ لِمَعْرِفَتِهِ بِمَا أَصَابَ السَّبْعَةَ الرِّجَالَ الَّذِينَ دَخَلُوا عَلَيْهَا، وَخَافَ أَنْ يُصِيبَ هذَا مَا أَصَابَهُمْ وَفِيمَا هُوَ مُتَرَدِّدٌ وَلَمْ يَرْدُدْ عَلَيْهِ جَوَاباً،
the Angel said to him: “Do not be afraid to give her to this one, because this one fears God. He is obliged to be joined to your daughter. Because of this, no other one could have her.”
the Angel said to him: “Do not be afraid to give her to this one, because this one fears God. He is obliged to be joined to your daughter. Because of this, no other one could have her.”
قَالَ لَهُ الْمَلاَكُ: «لاَ تَخَفْ أَنْ تُعْطِيَهَا لِهذَا، فَإِنَّ ابْنَتَكَ لَهُ يَنْبَغِي أَنْ تَكُونَ زَوْجَةً لأَنَّهُ يَخَافُ اللهَ، وَلِذلِكَ لَمْ يَقْدِرْ غَيْرُهُ أَنْ يَأْخُذَهَا».
Then Raguel said: “I do not doubt that God has admitted my prayers and tears before his sight.
Then Raguel said: “I do not doubt that God has admitted my prayers and tears before his sight.
حِينَئِذٍ قَالَ رَعُوئِيلُ: «لاَ أَشُكُّ أَنَّ اللهَ قَدْ تَقَبَّلَ صَلَوَاتِي وَدُمُوعِي أَمَامَهُ،

And I believe, therefore, that he has caused you to come to me, so that this one might be joined in marriage to one of her own kindred, according to the law of Moses. And now, do not continue to doubt that I will give her to you.”

And I believe, therefore, that he has caused you to come to me, so that this one might be joined in marriage to one of her own kindred, according to the law of Moses. And now, do not continue to doubt that I will give her to you.”
وَلَعَلَّهُ لأَجْلِ ذلِكَ سَاقَكُمَا اللهُ إِلَيَّ حَتَّى تَتَزَوَّجَ هذِهِ بِذِي قَرَابَتِهَا عَلَى حَسَبِ شَرِيعَةِ مُوسَى. وَالآنَ لاَ تَشُكَّ أَنِّي أُعْطِيكَهَا».

And taking the right hand of his daughter, he gave it into the right hand of Tobias, saying, “May the God of Abraham, and the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob be with you. And may he join you together in marriage and fulfill his blessing in you.”

And taking the right hand of his daughter, he gave it into the right hand of Tobias, saying, “May the God of Abraham, and the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob be with you. And may he join you together in marriage and fulfill his blessing in you.”

ثُمَّ أَخَذَ بِيَمِينِ ابْنَتِهِ سَارَةَ وَسَلَّمَهَا إِلَى يَمِينِ طُوبِيَّا، قَائِلاً: «إِلهُ إِبْرَاهِيمَ وَإِلهُ إِسْحَاقَ وَإِلهُ يَعْقُوبَ يَكُونُ مَعَكُمَا، وَهُوَ يَقْرِنُكُمَا وَيُتِمُّ بَرَكَتَهُ عَلَيْكُمَا».
And taking paper, they made a written record of the marriage.
And taking paper, they made a written record of the marriage.
ثُمَّ أَخَذُوا صَحِيفَةً وَكَتَبُوا فِيهَا عَقْدَ الزَّوَاجِ،
And after this, they feasted, blessing God.
And after this, they feasted, blessing God.
وَبَعْدَ ذلِكَ أَكَلُوا وَبَارَكُوا اللهَ.
And Raguel called his wife Anna to him, and he instructed her to prepare another bedroom.
And Raguel called his wife Anna to him, and he instructed her to prepare another bedroom.
وَدَعَا رَعُوئِيلُ حَنَّةَ زَوْجَتَهُ وَأَمَرَهَا أَنْ تُهَيِّءَ مُخْدَعاً آخَرَ،
And she brought her daughter Sarah into it, and she was weeping.
And she brought her daughter Sarah into it, and she was weeping.
وَأَدْخَلَتْهُ سَارَةَ ابْنَتَهَا وَهِيَ بَاكِيَةٌ،
And she said to her, “Be steadfast in spirit, my daughter. May the Lord of heaven give you gladness in place of the grief that you have had to endure.”
And she said to her, “Be steadfast in spirit, my daughter. May the Lord of heaven give you gladness in place of the grief that you have had to endure.”
وَقَالَتْ لَهَا: «تَشَجَّعِي يَا بُنَيَّةُ، وَرَبُّ السَّمَاءِ يُؤْتِيكِ فَرَحاً بَدَلَ الْغَمِّ الَّذِي قَاسَيْتِهِ».
In truth, after they had dined, they introduced the young man to her.
In truth, after they had dined, they introduced the young man to her.
وَلَمَّا فَرَغُوا مِنَ الْعَشَاءِ، أَدْخَلُوا عَلَيْهَا الْفَتَى،
And so, Tobias, remembering the word of the Angel, took part of the liver from his bag, and he placed it over the live coals.
And so, Tobias, remembering the word of the Angel, took part of the liver from his bag, and he placed it over the live coals.
فَذَكَرَ طُوبِيَّا كَلاَمَ الْمَلاَكِ، فَأَخْرَجَ مِنْ كِيسِهِ فِلْذَةً مِنَ الْكَبِدِ، وَأَلْقَاهَا عَلَى الْجَمْرِ الْمُشْتَعِلِ،
Then the Angel Raphael caught the demon, and bound him in the desert of upper Egypt.
Then the Angel Raphael caught the demon, and bound him in the desert of upper Egypt.
حِينَئِذٍ قَبَضَ الْمَلاَكُ رَافَائِيلُ عَلَى الشَّيْطَانِ وَأَوْثَقَهُ فِي بَرِّيَّةِ مِصْرَ الْعُلْيَا.

Then Tobias exhorted the virgin, and he said to her: “Sarah, get up and let us pray to God this day, and tomorrow, and the following day. For, during these three nights, we are being joined to God. And then, when the third night has passed, we ourselves will be joined together.

Then Tobias exhorted the virgin, and he said to her: “Sarah, get up and let us pray to God this day, and tomorrow, and the following day. For, during these three nights, we are being joined to God. And then, when the third night has passed, we ourselves will be joined together.

وَوَعَظَ طُوبِيَّا الْبِكْرَ وَقَالَ لَهَا: «يَا سَارَةُ قُومِي نُصَلِّي إِلَى اللهِ الْيَوْمَ وَغَدًا وَبَعْدَ غَدٍ، فَإِنَّا فِي هذِهِ اللَّيَالِي الثَّلاَثِ نَتَّحِدُ بِاللهِ وَبَعْدَ انْقِضَاءِ اللَّيْلَةِ الثَّالِثَةِ نَكُونُ فِي زَوَاجِنَا،
For certainly, we are the children of the saints, and we must not be joined together in such a manner as the heathens, who are ignorant of God.”
For certainly, we are the children of the saints, and we must not be joined together in such a manner as the heathens, who are ignorant of God.”
لأَنَّا بَنُو الْقِدِّيسِينَ فَلاَ يَنْبَغِي لَنَا أَنْ نَقْتَرِنَ اقْتِرَانَ الأُمَمِ الَّذِينَ لاَ يَعْرِفُونَ اللهَ».
And so, rising up together, they both prayed earnestly, at the same time, that health might be given to them.
And so, rising up together, they both prayed earnestly, at the same time, that health might be given to them.
فَقَامَا مَعاً وَصَلَّيَا كِلاَهُمَا بِحَرَارَةٍ حَتَّى يُعَافِيَهُمَا.
And Tobias said: “Lord, the God of our fathers, may the heavens and the earth bless you, and the sea, and the fountains, and the rivers, and all your creatures that are in them.
And Tobias said: “Lord, the God of our fathers, may the heavens and the earth bless you, and the sea, and the fountains, and the rivers, and all your creatures that are in them.
وَقَالَ طُوبِيَّا: «أَيُّهَا الرَّبُّ إِلهُ آبَائِنَا، لِتُبَارِكُكَ السَّمَوَاتُ وَالأَرْضُ وَالْبَحْرُ وَالْيَنَابِيعُ وَالأَنْهَارُ وَجَمِيعُ خَلاَئِقِكَ الَّتِي فِيهَا.
You formed Adam from the mud of the earth, and you gave Eve to him as a helper.
You formed Adam from the mud of the earth, and you gave Eve to him as a helper.
أَنْتَ جَبَلْتَ آدَمَ مِنْ تُرَابِ الأَرْضِ وَأَتَيْتَهُ حَوَّاءَ عَوْناً.
And now, O Lord, you know that I take my sister in conjugal union, not by reason of worldly pleasure, but solely for the love of posterity, in which your name may be blessed forever and ever.”
And now, O Lord, you know that I take my sister in conjugal union, not by reason of worldly pleasure, but solely for the love of posterity, in which your name may be blessed forever and ever.”
وَالآنَ يَا رَبِّ، أَنْتَ تَعْلَمُ أَنِّي لاَ لِسَبَبِ الشَّهْوَةِ أَتَّخِذُ أُخْتِي زَوْجَةً، وَإِنَّمَا رَغْبَةً فِي النَّسْلِ الَّذِي يُبَارَكُ فِيهِ اسْمُكَ إِلَى دَهْرِ الدُّهُورِ».
Sarah likewise said, “Be merciful to us, O Lord, be merciful to us. And let us both grow old together in health.”
Sarah likewise said, “Be merciful to us, O Lord, be merciful to us. And let us both grow old together in health.”
وَقَالَتْ سَارَةُ أَيْضاً: «ارْحَمْنَا يَا رَبِّ ارْحَمْنَا حَتَّى نَشِيخَ كِلاَنَا مَعاً فِي عَافِيَةٍ».
And it happened, about the time of the rooster’s crowing, that Raguel ordered his servants to be summoned, and they went out with him together to dig a grave.
And it happened, about the time of the rooster’s crowing, that Raguel ordered his servants to be summoned, and they went out with him together to dig a grave.
وَكَانَ نَحْوَ وَقْتِ صِيَاحِ الدِّيكِ، أَنَّ رَعُوئِيلَ أَمَرَ أَنْ يُجْمَعَ إِلَيْهِ غِلْمَانُهُ، فَانْطَلَقُوا مَعَهُ وَاحْتَفَرُوا قَبْراً،
For he said, “Lest perhaps, in the same way, it may have happened to him, as it did also to the other seven men who approached her.”
For he said, “Lest perhaps, in the same way, it may have happened to him, as it did also to the other seven men who approached her.”
لأَنَّهُ قَالَ: «أَخْشَى أَنْ يُصِيبَهُ مَا أَصَابَ غَيْرَهُ مِنَ الرِّجَالِ السَّبْعَةِ الَّذِينَ دَخَلُوا عَلَيْهَا».
And when they had prepared the pit, Raguel returned to his wife, and he said to her,
And when they had prepared the pit, Raguel returned to his wife, and he said to her,
فَلَمَّا أَعَدُّوا الْقَبْرَ، رَجَعَ رَعُوئِيلُ إِلَى زَوْجَتِهِ، وَقَالَ لَهَا:
“Send one of your maids, and let her see if he is dead, so that I may bury him before the dawn of day.”
“Send one of your maids, and let her see if he is dead, so that I may bury him before the dawn of day.”
«ابْعَثِي وَاحِدَةً مِنْ جَوَارِيكِ لِتَرَى هَلْ مَاتَ حَتَّى أُوَارِيَهُ قَبْلَ ضَوْءِ النَّهَارِ».
And so, she sent one of her maidservants, who entered the bedroom and discovered them safe and unharmed, sleeping both together.
And so, she sent one of her maidservants, who entered the bedroom and discovered them safe and unharmed, sleeping both together.
فَأَنْفَذَتْ إِحْدَى جَوَارِيهَا، فَدَخَلَتِ الْمُخْدَعَ، فَإِذَا هُمَا سَالِمَانِ مُعَافَيَانِ، وَهُمَا نَائِمَانِ مَعاً.
And returning, she reported the good news. And they blessed the Lord: Raguel, especially, and his wife Anna.
And returning, she reported the good news. And they blessed the Lord: Raguel, especially, and his wife Anna.
فَعَادَتْ وَأَخْبَرَتْ بِهذِهِ الْبُشْرَى. فَبَارَكَ رَعُوئِيلُ وَحَنَّةُ زَوْجَتُهُ الرَّبَّ
And they said: “We bless you, Lord God of Israel, because it has not happened in the way that we thought it might.
And they said: “We bless you, Lord God of Israel, because it has not happened in the way that we thought it might.
قَائِلَيْنِ: «نُبَارِكُكَ أَيُّهَا الرَّبُّ إِلهُ إِسْرَائِيلَ مِنْ أَجْلِ أَنَّهُ لَمْ يُصِبْنَا مَا كُنَّا نَتَوَقَّعُهُ،
For you have acted in your mercy toward us, and you have excluded from us the enemy who pursued us.
For you have acted in your mercy toward us, and you have excluded from us the enemy who pursued us.
فَإِنَّكَ قَدْ آتَيْتَنَا رَحْمَتَكَ وَحَبَسْتَ عَنَّا الْعَدُوَ الَّذِي يَضْطَهِدُنَا،

Moreover, you have had compassion on two only children. Make them, O Lord, able to bless you more fully and to offer you a sacrifice of your praise and of their health, so that all peoples everywhere may know that you alone are God in all the earth.”

Moreover, you have had compassion on two only children. Make them, O Lord, able to bless you more fully and to offer you a sacrifice of your praise and of their health, so that all peoples everywhere may know that you alone are God in all the earth.”

وَرَحِمْتَ الْوَحِيدَيْنِ. فَاجْعَلْهُمَا يَا رَبِّ يُبَارِكَانِكَ أَتَمَّ بَرَكَةٍ، وَيُقَدِّمَانِ لَكَ قُرْبَانَ تَسْبِيحِكَ وَعَافِيَتِهِمَا حَتَّى تَعْلَمَ الأُمَمُ كَافَّةً أَنَّكَ أَنْتَ الإِلهُ الْوَاحِدُ فِي الأَرْضِ كُلِّهَا».
And immediately Raguel instructed his servants to refill the pit, which they had made, before daylight.
And immediately Raguel instructed his servants to refill the pit, which they had made, before daylight.
وَلِلْحَالِ أَمَرَ رَعُوئِيلُ غِلْمَانَهُ أَنْ يَرْدِمُوا الْقَبْرَ الَّذِي حَفَرُوهُ قَبْلَ ضَوْءِ الصَّبَاحِ.
And then he told his wife to make ready a feast, and to prepare all the provisions that are necessary for those who undertake a journey.
And then he told his wife to make ready a feast, and to prepare all the provisions that are necessary for those who undertake a journey.
ثُمَّ أَوْعَزَ إِلَى زَوْجَتِهِ أَنْ تُعِدَّ وَلِيمَةً وَتُصْلِحَ مَا يَنْبَغِي لِلْمُسَافِرِينَ مِنَ الزَّادِ،
Likewise, he caused two fat cows and four rams to be killed, and a banquet to be prepared for all his neighbors and every one of his friends.
Likewise, he caused two fat cows and four rams to be killed, and a banquet to be prepared for all his neighbors and every one of his friends.
وَأَمَرَ بِذَبْحِ بَقَرَتَيْنِ سَمِينَتَيْنِ وَأَرْبَعَةِ أَكْبُشٍ، وَأَنْ تُهَيَّأَ وَلِيمَةٌ لِجَمِيعِ جِيرَانِهِ وَأَصْدِقَائِهِ.
And Raguel pleaded with Tobias to delay with him for two weeks.
And Raguel pleaded with Tobias to delay with him for two weeks.
وَاسْتَحْلَفَ رَعُوئِيلُ طُوبِيَّا أَنْ يُقِيمَ عِنْدَهُ أُسْبُوعَيْنِ.

Moreover, of all the things that Raguel possessed, he gave one half part to Tobias, and he made a writing, so that the half that remained should also pass into the ownership of Tobias, after their deaths.

Moreover, of all the things that Raguel possessed, he gave one half part to Tobias, and he made a writing, so that the half that remained should also pass into the ownership of Tobias, after their deaths.
وَأَعْطَى رَعُوئِيلُ لِطُوبِيَّا نِصْفَ مَالِهِ كُلِّهِ، وَكَتَبَ لِطُوبِيَّا صَكًّا بِالنِّصْفِ الْبَاقِي أَنْ يَسْتَوْلِيَ عَلَيْهِ بَعْدَ مَوْتِهِمَا.
Then Tobias called the Angel to him, whom he indeed considered to be a man, and he said to him: “Brother Azariah, I implore you to listen to my words:
Then Tobias called the Angel to him, whom he indeed considered to be a man, and he said to him: “Brother Azariah, I implore you to listen to my words:
ثُمَّ إِنَّ طُوبِيَّا اسْتَدْعَى الْمَلاَكَ الَّذِي كَانَ يَحْسَبُهُ إِنْسَاناً، وَقَالَ لَهُ: «يَا أَخِي عَزَرْيَا أَسْأَلُكَ أَنْ تَسْمَعَ كَلاَمِي:
If I should give myself to be your servant, I would not be equally worthy of your providence.
If I should give myself to be your servant, I would not be equally worthy of your providence.
«إِنِّي لَوْ جَعَلْتُ نَفْسِي عَبْدَا لَكَ لَمَا وَفَيْتُ بِعِنَايَتِكَ حَقَّ الْوَفَاءِ.

Even so, I beg you to take with you beasts or even servants, and to go to Gabael in Rages, the city of the Medes, and restore to him his handwritten note, and receive from him the money, and petition him to come to my wedding celebration.

Even so, I beg you to take with you beasts or even servants, and to go to Gabael in Rages, the city of the Medes, and restore to him his handwritten note, and receive from him the money, and petition him to come to my wedding celebration.

وَلكِنِّي مَعَ ذلِكَ أَسْأَلُكَ أَنْ تَأْخُذَ دَوَابَّ وَغِلْمَاناً، وَتَنْطَلِقَ إِلَى غَابِيلُوسَ فِي رَاجِيسَ مَدِينَةِ الْمَادِيِّينَ، وَتَرُدَّ عَلَيْهِ صَكَّهُ، وَتَقْبِضَ مِنْهُ الْفِضَّةَ، وَتَدْعُوَهُ إِلَى عُرْسِي،
For you know that my father numbers the days. And if I delay one day more, his soul will be afflicted.
For you know that my father numbers the days. And if I delay one day more, his soul will be afflicted.
لأَنَّكَ تَعْلَمُ أَنَّ أَبِي يَحْسُبُ الأَيَّامَ، فَإِنْ زِدْتُ فِي إِبْطَائِي يَوْماً وَاحِداً حَزِنَتْ نَفْسُهُ.
And surely you see how Raguel has obtained my oath, an oath that I am not able to spurn.”
And surely you see how Raguel has obtained my oath, an oath that I am not able to spurn.”
وَأَنْتَ تَرَى أَنَّ رَعُوئِيلَ قَدِ اسْتَحْلَفَنِي، وَلَسْتُ أَسْتَطِيعُ أَنْ أَسْتَخِفَّ بِحَلِفِهِ».

Then Raphael borrowed four of the servants of Raguel, and two camels, and he traveled to Rages, the city of the Medes. And upon finding Gabael, he gave him his handwritten note, and he received from him all the money.

Then Raphael borrowed four of the servants of Raguel, and two camels, and he traveled to Rages, the city of the Medes. And upon finding Gabael, he gave him his handwritten note, and he received from him all the money.

حِينَئِذٍ أَخَذَ رَافَائِيلُ أَرْبَعَةً مِنْ غِلْمَانِ رَعُوئِيلَ وَجَمَلَيْنِ وَسَافَرَ إِلَى رَاجِيسَ مَدِينَةِ الْمَادِيِّينَ وَلَقِيَ غَابِيلُوسَ، فَدَفَعَ إِلَيْهِ صَكَّهُ، وَاسْتَوْفَى مِنْهُ الْمَالَ كُلَّهُ،
And he revealed to him, concerning Tobias the son of Tobit, all that had been done. And he made him come with him to the wedding celebration.
And he revealed to him, concerning Tobias the son of Tobit, all that had been done. And he made him come with him to the wedding celebration.
وَعَرَّفَهُ أَمْرَ طُوبِيَّا بْنِ طُوبِيَّا وَكُلَّ مَا وَقَعَ، وَأَتَى بِهِ مَعَهُ إِلَى الْعُرْسِ.
And when he had entered into the house of Raguel, he discovered Tobias reclining at table. And leaping up, they kissed each other. And Gabael wept, and he blessed God.
And when he had entered into the house of Raguel, he discovered Tobias reclining at table. And leaping up, they kissed each other. And Gabael wept, and he blessed God.
فَلَمَّا دَخَلَ بَيْتَ رَعُوئِيلَ، وَجَدَ طُوبِيَّا مُتَّكِئاً، فَنَهَضَ قَائِماً، وَقَبَّلاَ بَعْضُهُمَا بَعْضاً، وَبَكَى غَابِيلُوسُ وَبَارَكَ اللهَ،
And he said: “May the God of Israel bless you, for you are the son of a man most noble and just, fearing God and performing almsgiving.
And he said: “May the God of Israel bless you, for you are the son of a man most noble and just, fearing God and performing almsgiving.
وَقَالَ: «يُبَارِكُكَ الرَّبُّ إِلهُ إِسْرَائِيلَ لأَنَّكَ ابْنُ رَجُلٍ صَالِحٍ جِدًّا بَارٍّ مُتَّقٍ للهِ صَانِعِ صَدَقَاتٍ.
And may a blessing be spoken over your wife and over your parents.
And may a blessing be spoken over your wife and over your parents.
وَتَحِلُّ الْبَرَكَةُ عَلَى زَوْجَتِكَ وَعَلَى وَالِدَيْكُمَا،
And may you see your sons, and the sons of your sons, even to the third and fourth generation. And may your offspring be blessed by the God of Israel, who reigns forever and ever.”
And may you see your sons, and the sons of your sons, even to the third and fourth generation. And may your offspring be blessed by the God of Israel, who reigns forever and ever.”
وَتَرَيَانِ بَنِيكُمَا وَبَنِي بَنِيكُمَا إِلَى الْجِيلِ الثَّالِثِ وَالرَّابِعِ، وَيَكُونُ نَسْلُكُمَا مُبَارَكاً مِنْ إِلهِ إِسْرَائِيلَ الْمَالِكِ إِلَى دَهْرِ الدُّهُورِ».
And when all had said, “Amen,” they approached the feast. But they also celebrated the marriage feast with the fear of the Lord.
And when all had said, “Amen,” they approached the feast. But they also celebrated the marriage feast with the fear of the Lord.
فَقَالُوا كُلُّهُمْ: «آمِينَ». ثُمَّ تَقَدَّمُوا إِلَى الْوَلِيمَةِ، إِلاَّ أَنَّهُمُ اتَّخَذُوا وَلِيمَةَ الْعُرْسِ بِخَوْفِ اللهِ.
In truth, when Tobias was delayed because of the marriage celebration, his father Tobit was worried, saying: “Why do you think my son is delayed, or why has he been detained there?
In truth, when Tobias was delayed because of the marriage celebration, his father Tobit was worried, saying: “Why do you think my son is delayed, or why has he been detained there?
وَلَمَّا أَبْطَأَ طُوبِيَّا هُنَاكَ لِسَبَبِ الْعُرْسِ، قَلِقَ أَبُوهُ طُوبِيَّا وَقَالَ: «لِمَاذَا تُرَى أَبْطَأَ ابْنِي؟ وَمَا الَّذِي عَاقَهُ هُنَاكَ؟
Do you think that Gabael has died, and that no one will repay him the money?”
Do you think that Gabael has died, and that no one will repay him the money?”
أَلَعَلَّ غَابِيلُوسَ قَدْ مَاتَ وَلَيْسَ مَنْ يَرُدُّ لَهُ الْمَالَ؟»
And so he began to be exceedingly sad, both he and his wife Anna with him. And they both began to weep together, because their son did not at least return to them on the appointed day.
And so he began to be exceedingly sad, both he and his wife Anna with him. And they both began to weep together, because their son did not at least return to them on the appointed day.
وَأَخَذَهُ حُزْنٌ شَدِيدٌ هُوَ وَحَنَّةَ امْرَأَتَهُ، وَطَفِقَا كِلاَهُمَا يَبْكِيَانِ لِتَخَلُّفِ ابْنِهِمَا عَنِ الرُّجُوعِ فِي يَوْمِ الْمِيعَادِ.

But his mother wept inconsolable tears, and also said: “Woe, woe to me, O my son. Why did we send you to journey far away, you: the light of our eyes, the staff of our old age, the solace of our life, the hope of our posterity?

But his mother wept inconsolable tears, and also said: “Woe, woe to me, O my son. Why did we send you to journey far away, you: the light of our eyes, the staff of our old age, the solace of our life, the hope of our posterity?

وَكَانَتْ أُمُّهُ تَبْكِي بِدُمُوعٍ لاَ تَنْقَطِعُ وَهِيَ تَقُولُ: «آهِ أَوْهِ يَا بُنَيَّ، لِمَاذَا أَرْسَلْنَاكَ فِي الْغُرْبَةِ يَا نُورَ أَبْصَارِنَا وَعُكَّازَةَ شَيْخُوخَتِنَا وَعَزَاءَ عِيشَتِنَا وَرَجَاءَ عَقِبِنَا؟
Having all things together as one in you, we ought not to have dismissed you from us.”
Having all things together as one in you, we ought not to have dismissed you from us.”
لَقَدْ كَانَ لَنَا فِيكَ وَحْدَكَ كُلُّ شَيْءٍ، فَلَمْ يَكُنْ يَنْبَغِي لَنَا أَنْ نُرْسِلَكَ عَنَّا».
And Tobit was saying to her: “Be calm, and do not be troubled. Our son is safe. That man, with whom we sent him, is faithful enough.”
And Tobit was saying to her: “Be calm, and do not be troubled. Our son is safe. That man, with whom we sent him, is faithful enough.”
فَكَانَ طُوبِيَّا يَقُولُ لَهَا: «اسْكُتِي وَلاَ تَتَقَلَّقِي، إِنَّ ابْنَنَا سَالِمٌ وَالرَّجُلَ الَّذِي أَرْسَلْنَاهُ مَعَهُ ثِقَةٌ جِدًّا».

Yet she was by no means able to be consoled. But, leaping up every day, she looked all round, and traveled around all the ways, by which there seemed any hope that he might return, so that she might possibly see him coming from afar.

Yet she was by no means able to be consoled. But, leaping up every day, she looked all round, and traveled around all the ways, by which there seemed any hope that he might return, so that she might possibly see him coming from afar.

فَلَمْ يَكُنْ ذلِكَ يُفِيدُهَا أَدْنَى تَعْزِيَةٍ، وَكَانَتْ كُلَّ يَوْمٍ تَقُومُ مُسْرِعَةً فَتَتَشَوَّفُ مِنْ كُلِّ جِهَةٍ، وَتَنْظُرُ فِي جَمِيعِ الطُّرُقِ الَّتِي كَانَتْ تَظُنُّ أَنَّ ابْنَهَا يَرْجِعُ مِنْهَا لَعَلَّهَا تَرَاهُ عَنْ بُعْدٍ مُقْبِلاً.
