In truth, Raguel said to his son-in-law, “Remain here, and I will send a message of your health to your father Tobit.”
In truth, Raguel said to his son-in-law, “Remain here, and I will send a message of your health to your father Tobit.”
وَأَمَّا رَعُوئِيلُ فَقَالَ لِصِهْرِهِ: «امْكُثْ ههُنَا وَأَنَا أُنْفِذُ إِلَى طُوبِيَّا أَبِيكَ مَنْ يُخْبِرُهُ بِسَلاَمَتِكَ».
And Tobias said to him, “I know that my father and my mother have now counted the days, and their spirit must be tortured within them.”
And Tobias said to him, “I know that my father and my mother have now counted the days, and their spirit must be tortured within them.”
فَقَالَ لَهُ طُوبِيَّا: «إِنِّي لأَعْلَمُ أَنَّ أَبِي وَأُمِّي يَحْسُبَانِ الأَيَّامِ وَأَرْوَاحُهُمَا مُعَذَّبَةٌ قَلَقاً».

And when Raguel had repeatedly petitioned Tobias, and he was by no means willing to listen to him, he delivered Sarah to him, and half of all his substance: with men and women servants, with sheep, camels, and cows, and with much money. And he dismissed him away, in safety and gladness, from him,

And when Raguel had repeatedly petitioned Tobias, and he was by no means willing to listen to him, he delivered Sarah to him, and half of all his substance: with men and women servants, with sheep, camels, and cows, and with much money. And he dismissed him away, in safety and gladness, from him,

وَبَعْدَ أَنْ أَكْثَرَ رَعُوئِيلُ مِنَ الإِلْحَاحِ عَلَى طُوبِيَّا، فَأَبَى أَنْ يَسْمَعَ بِوَجْهٍ مِنَ الْوُجُوهِ، أَعْطَاهُ سَارَةَ وَنِصْفَ أَمْوَالِهِ كُلِّهَا مِنْ غِلْمَانٍ وَجَوَارٍ وَمَوَاشٍ وَإِبِلٍ وَبَقَرٍ وَفِضَّةٍ كَثِيرَةٍ، وَصَرَفَهُ مِنْ عِنْدِهِ بِسَلاَمٍ فَرِحاً،

saying: “May the holy Angel of the Lord be with your journey, and may he lead you through unharmed, and may you discover that all is right concerning your parents, and may my eyes see your sons before I die.”

saying: “May the holy Angel of the Lord be with your journey, and may he lead you through unharmed, and may you discover that all is right concerning your parents, and may my eyes see your sons before I die.”
قَائِلاً: «مَلاَكُ الرَّبِّ الْقُدُّوسُ يَكُونُ فِي طَرِيقِكُمَا، وَيُبَلِّغُكُمَا سَالِمَيْنِ، وَتَجِدَانِ كُلَّ شَيْءٍ عِنْدَ أَبَوَيْكُمَا بِخَيْرٍ، وَتَرَى عَيْنَايَ بَنِيكُمَا قَبْلَ مَوْتِي».
And the parents, taking hold of their daughter, kissed her and let her go:
And the parents, taking hold of their daughter, kissed her and let her go:
وَأَقْبَلَ الْوَالِدَانِ عَلَى ابْنَتِهِمَا يُقَبِّلاَنِهَا ثُمَّ صَرَفَاهَا،
admonishing her to honor her father-in-law, to love her husband, to guide the family, to govern the household, and to behave irreproachably herself.
admonishing her to honor her father-in-law, to love her husband, to guide the family, to govern the household, and to behave irreproachably herself.
وَأَوْصَيَاهَا أَنْ تُكْرِمَ حَمَوَيْهَا، وَتُحِبَّ بَعْلَهَا، وَتُدَبِّرَ عِيَالَهَا، وَتَسُوسَ بَيْتَهَا، وَتَحْفَظَ نَفْسَهَا غَيْرَ مَلُومَةٍ.
And as they were returning, they came through to Haran, which is in the middle of the journey, opposite Nineveh, on the eleventh day.
And as they were returning, they came through to Haran, which is in the middle of the journey, opposite Nineveh, on the eleventh day.
وَفِيمَا هُمْ رَاجِعُونَ، وَقَدْ بَلَغُوا إِلَى حَارَانَ الَّتِي فِي وَسَطِ الطَّرِيقِ جِهَةَ نِينَوَى فِي الْيَوْمِ الْحَادِي عَشَرَ.
And the Angel said: “Brother Tobias, you know how you left behind your father.
And the Angel said: “Brother Tobias, you know how you left behind your father.
قَالَ الْمَلاَكُ: «يَا أَخِي طُوبِيَّا إِنَّكَ تَعْلَمُ كَيْفَ فَارَقْتَ أَبَاكَ.
And so, if it pleases you, let us go on ahead, and let the family follow after us with a slower step, together with your bride, and with the animals.”
And so, if it pleases you, let us go on ahead, and let the family follow after us with a slower step, together with your bride, and with the animals.”
فَلْنَتَقَدَّمْ نَحْنُ إِنْ أَحْبَبْتَ، وَالْعِيَالُ وَزَوْجَتُكَ يَلْحَقُونَنَا عَلَى مَهْلٍ مَعَ الْمَوَاشِي».
And since it pleased him to go on in this way, Raphael said to Tobias, “Take with you from the gall of the fish, for it will be necessary.” And so, Tobias took from its gall, and he went ahead.
And since it pleased him to go on in this way, Raphael said to Tobias, “Take with you from the gall of the fish, for it will be necessary.” And so, Tobias took from its gall, and he went ahead.
وَإِذْ تَوَافَقَا عَلَى الْمُضِيِّ، قَالَ رَافَائِيلُ لِطُوبِيَّا: «خُذْ مَعَكَ مِنْ مَرَارَةِ الْحُوتِ، فَإِنَّ لَنَا بِهَا حَاجَةً». فَأَخَذَ طُوبِيَّا مِنَ الْمَرَارَةِ، وَانْطَلَقَا.
But Anna sat beside the way every day, on the top of a hill, from where she would be able to see for a long distance.
But Anna sat beside the way every day, on the top of a hill, from where she would be able to see for a long distance.
وَأَمَّا حَنَّةُ فَكَانَتْ كُلَّ يَوْمٍ تَجْلِسُ عِنْدَ الطَّرِيقِ عَلَى رَأْسِ الْجَبَلِ حَيْثُ كَانَتْ تَسْتَطِيعُ أَنْ تَنْظُرَ عَلَى بُعْدٍ.

And while she was watching for his arrival from that place, she looked far off, and soon she realized that her son was approaching. And running, she reported it to her husband, saying: “Behold, your son arrives.”

And while she was watching for his arrival from that place, she looked far off, and soon she realized that her son was approaching. And running, she reported it to her husband, saying: “Behold, your son arrives.”

فَلَمَّا كَانَتْ تَتَشَوَّفُ ذَاتَ يَوْمٍ مِنْ ذلِكَ الْمَوْضِعِ، نَظَرَتْ عَلَى بُعْدٍ، وَلِلْوَقْتِ عَرَفَتْ أَنَّهُ ابْنُهَا قَادِماً، فَبَادَرَتْ وَأَخْبَرَتْ بَعْلَهَا قَائِلَةً: «هُوَذَا ابْنُكَ آتٍ».
And Raphael said to Tobias: “As soon as you enter into your house, immediately adore the Lord your God. And, giving thanks to him, approach your father, and kiss him.
And Raphael said to Tobias: “As soon as you enter into your house, immediately adore the Lord your God. And, giving thanks to him, approach your father, and kiss him.
وَقَالَ رَافَائِيلُ لِطُوبِيَّا: «إِذَا دَخَلْتَ بَيْتَكَ، فَاسْجُدْ فِي الْحَالِ لِلرَّبِّ إِلهِكَ وَاشْكُرْ لَهُ، ثُمَّ ادْنُ مِنْ أَبِيكَ وَقَبِّلْهُ،

And immediately anoint his eyes from this gall of the fish, which you carry with you. For you should know that his eyes will soon be opened, and your father will see the light of heaven, and he will rejoice at the sight of you.”

And immediately anoint his eyes from this gall of the fish, which you carry with you. For you should know that his eyes will soon be opened, and your father will see the light of heaven, and he will rejoice at the sight of you.”
وَاطْلِ لِسَاعَتِكَ عَيْنَيْهِ بِمَرَارَةِ الْحُوتِ هذِهِ الَّتِي مَعَكَ، وَاعْلَمْ أَنَّهُ لِلْحِينِ تَنْفَتِحُ عَيْنَاهُ، وَيَرَى أَبُوكَ ضَوْءَ السَّمَاءِ، وَيَفْرَحُ بِرُؤْيَتِكَ».
Then the dog, which had been with them in the way, ran ahead, and, arriving like a messenger, he showed his joy by fawning and wagging his tail.
Then the dog, which had been with them in the way, ran ahead, and, arriving like a messenger, he showed his joy by fawning and wagging his tail.
حِينَئِذٍ سَبَقَ الْكَلْبُ الَّذِي كَانَ مَعَهُ فِي الطَّرِيقِ، وَكَانَ كَأَنَّهُ بَشِيرٌ يُبْدِي مَسَرَّتَهُ بِبَصْبَصَةِ ذَنَبِهِ.
And rising up, his blind father began to run, stumbling with his feet. And giving his hand to a servant, he ran on to meet his son.
And rising up, his blind father began to run, stumbling with his feet. And giving his hand to a servant, he ran on to meet his son.
فَقَامَ أَبُوهُ وَهُوَ أَعْمَى وَجَعَلَ يجْرِي وَهُوَ يَتَعَثَّرُ بِرِجْلَيْهِ، فَنَاوَلَ يَدَهُ لِغُلاَمٍ وَخَرَجَ لِمُلاَقَاةِ ابْنِهِ
And receiving him, he kissed him, as did his wife, and they both began to weep for joy.
And receiving him, he kissed him, as did his wife, and they both began to weep for joy.
وَاسْتَقْبَلَهُ وَقَبَّلَهُ هُوَ وَامْرَأَتُهُ، وَطَفِقَا كِلاَهُمَا يَبْكِيَانِ مِنَ الْفَرَحِ.
And when they had adored God and had given thanks, they sat down together.
And when they had adored God and had given thanks, they sat down together.
ثُمَّ سَجَدُوا للهِ وَشَكَرُوا لَهُ وَجَلَسُوا.
Then Tobias, taking from the gall of the fish, anointed his father’s eyes.
Then Tobias, taking from the gall of the fish, anointed his father’s eyes.
فَأَخَذَ طُوبِيَّا مِنْ مَرَارَةِ الْحُوتِ وَطَلَى عَيْنَيْ أَبِيهِ
And about half an hour passed, and then a white film began to come out of his eyes, like the membrane of an egg.
And about half an hour passed, and then a white film began to come out of his eyes, like the membrane of an egg.
وَمَكَثَ مِقْدَارَ نِصْفِ سَاعَةٍ، فَبَدَأَ يَخْرُجُ مِنْ عَيْنَيْهِ غِشَاوَةٌ كَغِرْقِئ الْبَيْضِ.
So, taking hold of it, Tobias pulled it away from his eyes, and immediately he received his sight.
So, taking hold of it, Tobias pulled it away from his eyes, and immediately he received his sight.
فَأَمْسَكَهَا طُوبِيَّا وَسَحَبَهَا مِنْ عَيْنَيْهِ، وَلِلْوَقْتِ عَادَ إِلَى طُوبِيَّا بَصَرُهُ.
And they glorified God: Tobit especially, and his wife, and all those who knew him.
And they glorified God: Tobit especially, and his wife, and all those who knew him.
فَمَجَّدَ اللهَ هُوَ وَامْرَأَتُهُ وَكُلُّ مَنْ كَانَ يَعْرِفُهُ.
And Tobit said, “I bless you, O Lord God of Israel, because you have chastised me, and you have saved me, and behold, I see my son Tobias.”
And Tobit said, “I bless you, O Lord God of Israel, because you have chastised me, and you have saved me, and behold, I see my son Tobias.”
وَقَالَ طُوبِيَّا: «أُبَارِكُكَ أَيُّهَا الرَّبُّ إِلهُ إِسْرَائِيلَ، لأَنَّكَ أَدَّبْتَنِي وَشَفَيْتَنِي، وَهَأَنَذَا أَرَى طُوبِيَّا وَلَدِي».

And then, after seven days, Sarah, the wife of his son, and all the family arrived safely, along with the sheep, and the camels, and much money from his wife, but also with that money which he had received from Gabael.

And then, after seven days, Sarah, the wife of his son, and all the family arrived safely, along with the sheep, and the camels, and much money from his wife, but also with that money which he had received from Gabael.

وَأَمَّا سَارَةُ كَنَّتُهُ فَوَصَلَتْ بَعْدَ سَبْعَةِ أَيَّامٍ هِيَ وَجَمِيعُ الْعِيَالِ بِسَلاَمٍ وَالْغَنَمُ وَالإِبِلُ وَمَالٌ كَثِيرٌ مِمَّا لِلْمَرْأَةِ مَعَ الْمَالِ الَّذِي اسْتَوْفَاهُ مِنْ غَابِيلُوسَ.
And he explained to his parents all the benefits from God, which he had produced all around him, by means of the man who had led him.
And he explained to his parents all the benefits from God, which he had produced all around him, by means of the man who had led him.
وَأَخْبَرَ أَبَوَيْهِ بِجَمِيعِ إِحْسَانَاتِ اللهِ الَّتِي أَنْعَمَ بِهَا عَلَيْهِ عَلَى يَدِ ذلِكَ الرَّجُلِ الَّذِي ذَهَبَ مَعَهُ.
And then Ahikar and Nadab arrived, the maternal first cousins of Tobias, rejoicing for Tobias, and congratulating with him for all the good things that God had revealed all around him.
And then Ahikar and Nadab arrived, the maternal first cousins of Tobias, rejoicing for Tobias, and congratulating with him for all the good things that God had revealed all around him.
وَوَفَدَ عَلَى طُوبِيَّا أَحْيُورُ وَنَبَاطُ وَهُمَا ذُوَا قَرَابَةٍ لَهُ فَرِحَيْنِ وَهَنَّآهُ بِجَمِيعِ مَا مَنَّ اللهُ بِهِ عَلَيْهِ مِنَ الْخَيْرِ
And for seven days they feasted, and all were rejoicing with great joy.
And for seven days they feasted, and all were rejoicing with great joy.
وَعَمِلُوا وَلِيمَةً سَبْعَةَ أَيَّامٍ، وَفَرِحُوا كُلُّهُمْ فَرَحاً عَظِيماً.
Then Tobit called his son to him, and he said to him, “What are we able to give to this holy man, who accompanied you?”
Then Tobit called his son to him, and he said to him, “What are we able to give to this holy man, who accompanied you?”
حِينَئِذٍ دَعَا طُوبِيَّا ابْنَهُ إِلَيْهِ وَقَالَ لَهُ: «مَاذَا تُرَى نُعْطِي هذَا الرَّجُلَ الْقِدِّيسَ الَّذِي ذَهَبَ مَعَكَ؟»
Tobias, answering, said to his father: “Father, what wages shall we give him? And what could be worthy of his benefits?
Tobias, answering, said to his father: “Father, what wages shall we give him? And what could be worthy of his benefits?
فَأَجَابَ طُوبِيَّا وَقَالَ لأَبِيهِ: «يَا أَبَتِ، أَيَّ أُجْرَةٍ نُعْطِيهِ، وَأَيُّ شَيْءٍ يَكُونُ مُؤَازِياً لإِحْسَانِهِ؟

He led me and he brought me back safely. He received the money from Gabael. He caused me to have my wife. And he confined the demon away from her. He caused joy to her parents. Myself, he rescued from being devoured by the fish. As for you, he also caused you to see the light of heaven. And so, we have been filled with all good things through him. What could we possibly give to him that would be worthy of these things?

He led me and he brought me back safely. He received the money from Gabael. He caused me to have my wife. And he confined the demon away from her. He caused joy to her parents. Myself, he rescued from being devoured by the fish. As for you, he also caused you to see the light of heaven. And so, we have been filled with all good things through him. What could we possibly give to him that would be worthy of these things?

أَخَذَنِي وَرَجَعَ بِي سَالِماً، وَالْمَالَ هُوَ اسْتَوْفَاهُ مِنْ عِنْدِ غَابِيلُوسَ، وَبِهِ حَصَلْتُ عَلَى زَوْجَتِي، وَهُوَ كُفَّ عَنْهَا الشَّيْطَانَ، وَفَرَّحَ أَبَوَيْهَا، وَخَلَّصَنِي مِنِ افْتِرَاسِ الْحُوتِ، وَإِيَّاكَ أَيْضاً هُوَ جَعَلَكَ تُبْصِرُ نُورَ السَّمَاءِ، وَبِهِ غُمِرْنَا بِكُلِّ خَيْرٍ. فَمَاذَا عَسَى أَنْ نُعْطِيَهُ مِمَّا يَكُونُ مُؤَازِياً لِهذِهِ؟
But I implore you, my father, to ask him if he would perhaps deign to take for himself half of all the things that have been brought.”
But I implore you, my father, to ask him if he would perhaps deign to take for himself half of all the things that have been brought.”
لكِنِّي أَسْأَلُكَ يَا أَبَتِ، أَنْ تَسْأَلْهُ هَلْ يَرْضَى أَنْ يَأْخُذَ النِّصْفَ مِنْ كُلِّ مَا جِئْنَا بِهِ؟»

And calling him, the father especially, and the son, they took him aside. And they began to petition him, so that he would deign to accept ownership of one half part of all things that they had brought.

And calling him, the father especially, and the son, they took him aside. And they began to petition him, so that he would deign to accept ownership of one half part of all things that they had brought.
فَدَعَاهُ الْوَالِدُ وَوَلَدُهُ وَأَخَذَاهُ نَاحِيَةً وَجَعَلاَ يَسْأَلاَنِهِ أَنْ يَتَنَازَلَ وَيَقْبَلَ النِّصْفَ مِنْ جَمِيعِ مَا جَاءَا بِهِ.
Then he said to them privately: “Bless the God of heaven, and confess to him in the sight of all who live, for he has acted in his mercy toward you.
Then he said to them privately: “Bless the God of heaven, and confess to him in the sight of all who live, for he has acted in his mercy toward you.
حِينَئِذٍ خَاطَبَهُمَا سِرًّا وَقَالَ: «بَارِكَا إِلهَ السَّمَاءِ وَاعْتَرِفَا لَهُ أَمَامَ جَمِيعِ الأَحْيَاءِ لِمَا آتَاكُمَا مِنْ مَرَاحِمِهِ.
For it is good to conceal the secret of a king, just as it is also honorable to reveal and to confess the works of God.
For it is good to conceal the secret of a king, just as it is also honorable to reveal and to confess the works of God.
أَمَّا سِرُّ الْمَلِكِ فَخَيْرٌ أَنْ يُكْتَمَ، وَأَمَّا أَعْمَالُ اللهِ فَإِذَاعَتُهَا وَالاِعْتِرَافُ بِهَا كَرَامَةٌ.
Prayer with fasting is good, and almsgiving is better than hiding away gold in storage.
Prayer with fasting is good, and almsgiving is better than hiding away gold in storage.
صَالِحَةٌ الصَّلاَةُ مَعَ الصَّوْمِ، وَالصَّدَقَةُ خَيْرٌ مِنِ ادِّخَارِ كُنُوزِ الذَّهَبِ.
For almsgiving delivers from death, and the same is what purges sins and makes one able to find mercy and everlasting life.
For almsgiving delivers from death, and the same is what purges sins and makes one able to find mercy and everlasting life.
لأَنَّ الصَّدَقَةَ تُنَجِّي مِنَ الْمَوْتِ وَتَمْحُو الْخَطَايَا وَتُؤَهِّلُ الإِنْسَانَ لِنَوَالِ الرَّحْمَةِ وَالْحَيَاةِ الأَبَدِيَّةِ
But those who commit sin and iniquity are enemies to their own soul.
But those who commit sin and iniquity are enemies to their own soul.
وَأَمَّا الَّذِينَ يَعْمَلُونَ الْمَعْصِيَةَ وَالإِثْمَ فَهُمْ أَعْدَاءٌ لأَنْفُسِهِمْ.
Therefore, I reveal the truth to you, and I will not hide the explanation from you.
Therefore, I reveal the truth to you, and I will not hide the explanation from you.
أَمَّا أَنَا فَأُعْلِنُ لَكُمَا الْحَقَّ وَمَا أَكْتُمُ عَنْكُمَا أَمْراً مَسْتُوراً.
When you prayed with tears, and buried the dead, and left behind your dinner, and hid the dead by day in your house, and buried them by night: I offered your prayer to the Lord.
When you prayed with tears, and buried the dead, and left behind your dinner, and hid the dead by day in your house, and buried them by night: I offered your prayer to the Lord.
إِنَّكَ حِينَ كُنْتَ تُصَلِّي بِدُمُوعٍ وَتَدْفِنُ الْمَوْتَى وَتَتْرُكُ طَعَامَكَ وَتَخْبَأُ الْمَوْتَى فِي بَيْتِكَ نَهَاراً وَتَدْفِنُهُمْ لَيْلاً، كُنْتُ أَنَا أَرْفَعُ صَلاَتَكَ إِلَى الرَّبِّ.
And because you were acceptable to God, it was necessary for you to be tested by trials.
And because you were acceptable to God, it was necessary for you to be tested by trials.
وَإِذْ كُنْتَ مَقْبُولاً أَمَامَ اللهِ كَانَ لاَ بُدَّ أَنْ تُمْتَحَنَ بِتَجْرِبَةٍ.
And now, the Lord has sent me to cure you, and to free Sarah, your son’s wife, from the demon.
And now, the Lord has sent me to cure you, and to free Sarah, your son’s wife, from the demon.
وَالآنَ فَإِنَّ الرَّبَّ قَدْ أَرْسَلَنِي لأَشْفِيَكَ، وَأُخَلِّصَ سَارَةَ كَنَّتَكَ مِنَ الشَّيْطَانِ.
For I am the Angel Raphael, one of seven, who stand before the Lord.”
For I am the Angel Raphael, one of seven, who stand before the Lord.”
فَإِنِّي أَنَا رَافَائِيلُ الْمَلاَكُ أَحَدُ السَّبْعَةِ الْوَاقِفِينَ أَمَامَ الرَّبِّ».
And when they had heard these things, they were troubled, and being seized with fear, they fell upon the ground on their face.
And when they had heard these things, they were troubled, and being seized with fear, they fell upon the ground on their face.
فَلَمَّا سَمِعَا مَقَالَتَهُ هذِهِ، ارْتَاعَا وَسَقَطَا عَلَى أَوْجُهِهِمَا عَلَى الأَرْضِ مُرْتَعِدَيْنِ.
And the Angel said to them: “Peace be to you. Fear not.
And the Angel said to them: “Peace be to you. Fear not.
فَقَالَ لَهُمَا الْمَلاَكُ: «سَلاَمٌ لَكُمْ، لاَ تَخَافُوا،
For when I was with you, I was there by the will of God. Bless him, and sing to him.
For when I was with you, I was there by the will of God. Bless him, and sing to him.
لأَنِّي لَمَّا كُنْتُ مَعَكُمْ إِنَّمَا كُنْتُ بِمَشِيئَةِ اللهِ، فَبَارِكُوهُ وَسَبِّحُوهُ.
Indeed, I seemed to eat and drink with you, but I make use of an invisible food and drink, which cannot be seen by men.
Indeed, I seemed to eat and drink with you, but I make use of an invisible food and drink, which cannot be seen by men.
وَكَانَ يَظْهَرُ لَكُمْ أَنِّي آكُلُ وَأَشْرَبُ مَعَكُمْ، وَإِنَّمَا أَنَا أَتَّخِذُ طَعَاماً غَيْرَ مَنْظُورٍ وَشَرَاباً لاَ يُبْصِرُهُ بَشَرٌ.
Therefore, it is time that I return to him who sent me. But as for you, bless God, and describe all his wonders.”
Therefore, it is time that I return to him who sent me. But as for you, bless God, and describe all his wonders.”
وَالآنَ قَدْ حَانَ أَنْ أَرْجِعَ إِلَى مَنْ أَرْسَلَنِي، وَأَنْتُمْ فَبَارِكُوا اللهَ وَحَدِّثُوا بِجَمِيعِ عَجَائِبِهِ».
And when he had said these things, he was taken from their sight, and they were not able to see him any longer.
And when he had said these things, he was taken from their sight, and they were not able to see him any longer.
وَبَعْدَ أَنْ قَالَ هذَا، ارْتَفَعَ عَنْ أَبْصَارِهِمْ، فَلَمْ يَعُودُوا يُعَايِنُونَهُ بَعْدَ ذلِكَ.
Then, lying prostrate for three hours upon their face, they blessed God. And rising up, they described all his wonders.
Then, lying prostrate for three hours upon their face, they blessed God. And rising up, they described all his wonders.
حِينَئِذٍ لَبِثُوا ثَلاَثَ سَاعَاتٍ مُنْطَرِحِينَ عَلَى وُجُوهِهِمْ يُبَارِكُونَ اللهَ، ثُمَّ نَهَضُوا وَحَدَّثُوا بِجَمِيعِ عَجَائِبِهِ.
And so, the elder Tobit, opening his mouth, blessed the Lord, and he said: “O Lord, you are great in eternity and your kingdom is with all ages.
And so, the elder Tobit, opening his mouth, blessed the Lord, and he said: “O Lord, you are great in eternity and your kingdom is with all ages.
حِينَئِذٍ فَتَحَ طُوبِيَّا الشَّيْخُ فَاهُ مُبَارِكاً لِلرَّبِّ وَقَالَ: «عَظِيمٌ أَنْتَ يَا رَبُّ إِلَى الأَبَدِ وَفِي جَمِيعِ الدُّهُورِ مُلْكُكَ،
For you scourge, and you save. You lead down to the grave, and you bring up again. And there is no one who can escape from your hand.
For you scourge, and you save. You lead down to the grave, and you bring up again. And there is no one who can escape from your hand.
لأَنَّكَ تَجْرَحُ وَتَشْفِي وَتُحْدِرُ إِلَى الْجَحِيمِ وَتُصْعِدُ مِنْهُ، وَلَيْسَ مَنْ يَفِرُّ مِنْ يَدِكَ.
Confess to the Lord, O sons of Israel, and praise him in the sight of the nations.
Confess to the Lord, O sons of Israel, and praise him in the sight of the nations.
اِعْتَرِفُوا لِلرَّبِّ يَا بَنِي إِسْرَائِيلَ وَسَبِّحُوهُ أَمَامَ جَمِيعِ الأُمَمِ،
For, indeed, he has dispersed you among the Gentiles, who are ignorant of him, that you may proclaim his wonders, and that you may cause them to know that there is no other almighty God, except him.
For, indeed, he has dispersed you among the Gentiles, who are ignorant of him, that you may proclaim his wonders, and that you may cause them to know that there is no other almighty God, except him.
فَإِنَّهُ فَرَّقَكُمْ بَيْنَ الأُمَمِ الَّذِينَ يَجْهَلُونَهُ لِكَيْ تُخْبِرُوا بِمُعْجِزَاتِهِ وَتُعَرِّفُوهُمْ أَنْ لاَ إِلهَ قَادِراً عَلَى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ سِوَاهُ.
He has chastised us because of our iniquities, and he will save us because of his mercy.
He has chastised us because of our iniquities, and he will save us because of his mercy.
هُوَ أَدَّبَنَا لأَجْلِ آثَامِنَا، وَهُوَ يُخَلِّصُنَا لأَجْلِ رَحْمَتِهِ.
Therefore, look upon what he has done for us, and, with fear and trembling, confess to him. And extol the King of all ages with your works.
Therefore, look upon what he has done for us, and, with fear and trembling, confess to him. And extol the King of all ages with your works.
اُنْظُرُوا الآنَ مَا صَنَعَ لَنَا، وَاعْتَرِفُوا لَهُ بِخَوْفٍ وَرِعْدَةٍ، وَمَجِّدُوا مَلِكَ الدُّهُورِ بِأَعْمَالِكُمْ.
But as for me, I will confess him in the land of my captivity. For he has revealed his majesty within a sinful nation.
But as for me, I will confess him in the land of my captivity. For he has revealed his majesty within a sinful nation.
أَمَّا أَنَا فَفِي أَرْضِ جَلاَئِي أَعْتَرِفُ لَهُ، لأَنَّهُ أَظْهَرَ جَلاَلَهُ فِي أُمَّةٍ خَاطِئَةٍ.
And so, be converted, you sinners, and do justice in the presence of God, believing that he will act in his mercy toward you.
And so, be converted, you sinners, and do justice in the presence of God, believing that he will act in his mercy toward you.
اِرْجِعُوا الآنَ أَيُّهَا الْخُطَاةُ وَاصْنَعُوا أَمَامَ اللهِ بِرًّا، وَاثِقِينَ بِأَنَّهُ يَصْنَعُ لَكُمْ رَحْمَةً.
But I and my soul will rejoice in him.
But I and my soul will rejoice in him.
أَمَّا أَنَا فَنَفْسِي تَتَهَلَّلُ بِهِ.
Bless the Lord, all you his elect. Keep days of rejoicing, and confess to him.
Bless the Lord, all you his elect. Keep days of rejoicing, and confess to him.
بَارِكُوا الرَّبَّ يَا جَمِيعَ مُخْتَارِيهِ، أَقِيمُوا أَيَّامَ فَرَحٍ وَاعْتَرِفُوا لَهُ.
O Jerusalem, the city of God, the Lord has chastised you for the works of your hands.
O Jerusalem, the city of God, the Lord has chastised you for the works of your hands.
يَا أُورُشَلِيمُ مَدِينَةَ اللهِ إِنَّ الرَّبَّ أَدَّبَكِ بِأَعْمَالِ يَدَيْكِ.

Confess to the Lord with your good things, and bless the God of all ages, so that he may rebuild his tabernacle in you, and he may recall all the captives to you, and you may be glad in all ages and forever.

Confess to the Lord with your good things, and bless the God of all ages, so that he may rebuild his tabernacle in you, and he may recall all the captives to you, and you may be glad in all ages and forever.
اُشْكُرِي للهِ نِعْمَتَهُ عَلَيْكِ، وَبَارِكِي إِلهَ الدُّهُورِ حَتَّى يَعُودَ فَيُشَيِّدَ مَسْكِنَهُ فِيكِ، وَيَرُدَّ إِلَيْكِ جَمِيعَ أَهْلِ الْجَلاَءِ، وَتَبْتَهِجِي إِلَى دَهْرِ الدُّهُورِ.
You will shine with a splendid light, and all the ends of the earth will adore by you.
You will shine with a splendid light, and all the ends of the earth will adore by you.
تَتَلأْلَئِينَ بِسَنًى بَهِيجٍ وَجَمِيعُ شُعُوبِ الأَرْضِ لَكِ يَسْجُدُونَ.
Nations from far away will come to you, bringing gifts. And in you, they shall adore the Lord, and they will hold your land in sanctification.
Nations from far away will come to you, bringing gifts. And in you, they shall adore the Lord, and they will hold your land in sanctification.
يَزُورُكِ الأُمَمُ مِنَ الأَقَاصِي بِقَرَابِينِهِمْ وَيَسْجُدُونَ فِيكِ لِلرَّبِّ وَيَعْتَدُّونَ أَرْضَكِ أَرْضاً مُقَدَّسَةً،
For they will invoke the Great Name in you.
For they will invoke the Great Name in you.
لأَنَّهُمْ فِيكِ يَدْعُونَ الاِسْمَ الْعَظِيمَ.
Those who despise you will be cursed, and all those who blaspheme by you will be condemned, and those who build you up will be blessed.
Those who despise you will be cursed, and all those who blaspheme by you will be condemned, and those who build you up will be blessed.
مَلْعُونِينَ يَكُونُونَ الَّذِينَ اسْتَهَانُوا بِكِ، وَالَّذِينَ جَدَّفُوا عَلَيْكِ يُدَانُونَ، وَيُبَارَكُ الَّذِينَ يَبْنُونَكِ.
But you will rejoice in your sons, because they will all be blessed, and they will be gathered together for the Lord.
But you will rejoice in your sons, because they will all be blessed, and they will be gathered together for the Lord.
أَمَّا أَنْتِ فَتَفْرَحِينَ بِبَنِيكِ، لأَنَّهُمْ يُبَارَكُونَ كَافَّةً، وَإِلَى الرَّبِّ يَحْتَشِدُونَ.
Blessed are all those who love you and who rejoice in your peace.
Blessed are all those who love you and who rejoice in your peace.
طُوبَى لِلَّذِينَ يُحِبُّونَكِ وَيَفْرَحُونَ لَكِ بِالسَّلاَمِ.
Bless the Lord, O my soul, for the Lord our God has freed Jerusalem, his city, from every one of her tribulations.
Bless the Lord, O my soul, for the Lord our God has freed Jerusalem, his city, from every one of her tribulations.
بَارِكِي يَا نَفْسِي الرَّبَّ، لأَنَّ الرَّبَّ إِلهَنَا خَلَّصَ أُورُشَلِيمَ مَدِينَتَهُ مِنْ جَمِيعِ شَدَائِدِهَا.
Happy will I be, if any of my offspring will be left to see the brightness of Jerusalem.
Happy will I be, if any of my offspring will be left to see the brightness of Jerusalem.
طُوبَى لِي إِنْ بَقِيَ مِنْ ذُرِّيَّتِي مَنْ يُبْصِرُ بَهَاءَ أُورُشَلِيمَ.
The gates of Jerusalem will be built from sapphire and emerald, and all its walls will be surrounded with precious stones.
The gates of Jerusalem will be built from sapphire and emerald, and all its walls will be surrounded with precious stones.
أَبْوَابُ أُورُشَلِيمَ مِنْ يَاقُوتٍ وَزُمُرُّدٍ وَكُلُّ مُحِيطِ أَسْوَارِهَا مِنْ حَجَرٍ كَرِيمٍ،
All its streets will be paved with stones, white and clean. And ‘Alleluia’ will be sung throughout its neighborhoods.
All its streets will be paved with stones, white and clean. And ‘Alleluia’ will be sung throughout its neighborhoods.
وَجَمِيعُ أَسْوَاقِهَا مَفْرُوشَةٌ بِحَجَرٍ أَبْيَضَ نَقِيٍّ، وَفِي شَوَارِعِهَا يُنْشَدُ هَلِّلُويَا.
Blessed be the Lord, who has exalted it, and may he reign over it, forever and ever. Amen.”
Blessed be the Lord, who has exalted it, and may he reign over it, forever and ever. Amen.”
مُبَارَكٌ الرَّبُّ الَّذِي عَظَّمَهَا، وَلِيَكُنْ مُلْكُهُ فِيهَا إِلَى دَهْرِ الدُّهُورِ. آمِينَ».
And the sermon of Tobit was completed. And after Tobit received his sight, he lived forty-two years, and he saw the sons of his grandchildren.
And the sermon of Tobit was completed. And after Tobit received his sight, he lived forty-two years, and he saw the sons of his grandchildren.
وَفَرَغَ طُوبِيَّا مِنْ كَلاَمِهِ وَعَاشَ طُوبِيَّا بَعْدَمَا عَادَ بَصِيراً اثْنَتَيْنِ وَأَرْبَعِينَ سَنَةً، وَرَأَى بَنِي حَفَدَتِهِ.
And so, having completed one hundred and two years, he was buried honorably at Nineveh.
And so, having completed one hundred and two years, he was buried honorably at Nineveh.
فَتَمَّتْ سِنُوهُ مِئَةً وَاثْنَتَيْنِ وَدُفِنَ بِكَرَامَةٍ فِي نِينَوَى.
For he was fifty-six years old, when he lost the light of his eyes, and he was sixty years old, when he truly received it again.
For he was fifty-six years old, when he lost the light of his eyes, and he was sixty years old, when he truly received it again.
وَكَانَ حِينَ ذَهَبَ بَصَرُهُ ابْنَ سِتَّ وَخَمْسِينَ سَنَةً، وَعَادَ يُبْصِرُ وَهُوَ ابْنُ سِتِّينَ سَنَةً،
And, in truth, the remainder of his life was in gladness. And so, with the good accomplishment of the fear of God, he departed in peace.
And, in truth, the remainder of his life was in gladness. And so, with the good accomplishment of the fear of God, he departed in peace.
وَقَضَى بَقِيَّةَ حَيَاتِهِ مَسْرُوراً. وَإِذْ بَلَغَ مِنْ تَقْوَى اللهِ غَايَةً حَسَنَةً، انْتَقَلَ بِسَلاَمٍ.
But, in the hour of his death, he called to himself his son Tobias, along with his sons, the seven youths who were his grandsons, and he said to them:
But, in the hour of his death, he called to himself his son Tobias, along with his sons, the seven youths who were his grandsons, and he said to them:
وَلَمَّا حَضْرَتْهُ الْوَفَاةُ، دَعَا ابْنَهُ طُوبِيَّا وَبَنِي ابْنِهِ السَّبْعَةَ الْفِتْيَانَ وَقَالَ لَهُمْ:
“Nineveh will pass away soon. For the word of the Lord goes forward, and our brothers, who have been dispersed away from the land of Israel, shall return to it.
“Nineveh will pass away soon. For the word of the Lord goes forward, and our brothers, who have been dispersed away from the land of Israel, shall return to it.
«قَدْ دَنَا دَمَارُ نِينَوَى، لأَنَّ كَلاَمَ الرَّبِّ لاَ يَذْهَبُ بَاطِلاً، وَإِخْوَتُنَا الَّذِينَ تَفَرَّقُوا مِنْ أَرْضِ إِسْرَائِيلَ يَرْجِعُونَ إِلَيْهَا،
Thus its deserted land will be entirely filled again. And the house of God, which was burned like incense within it, will be rebuilt again. And all those who fear God will return there.
Thus its deserted land will be entirely filled again. And the house of God, which was burned like incense within it, will be rebuilt again. And all those who fear God will return there.
وَكُلُّ أَرْضِهَا الْمُقْفِرَةِ سَتَمْتَلِئُ، وَبَيْتُ اللهِ الَّذِي أُحْرِقَ فِيهَا سَيُسْتَأْنَفُ بِنَاؤُهُ، وَسَيَرْجِعُ إِلَى هُنَاكَ جَمِيعُ خَائِفِي اللهِ.
And the Gentiles will relinquish their idols, and they will enter into Jerusalem, and they will dwell in it.
And the Gentiles will relinquish their idols, and they will enter into Jerusalem, and they will dwell in it.
وَسَتَتْرُكُ الأُمَمُ أَصْنَامَهَا وَتَرْحَلُ إِلَى أُورُشَلِيمَ فَتُقِيمُ بِهَا.
And all the kings of the earth will rejoice in it, adoring the King of Israel.
And all the kings of the earth will rejoice in it, adoring the King of Israel.
وَتَفْرَحُ فِيهَا مُلُوكُ الأَرْضِ كَافَّةً سَاجِدَةً لِمَلِكِ إِسْرَائِيلَ.
Therefore, my sons, listen to your father. Serve the Lord in truth, and seek to do the things that please him.
Therefore, my sons, listen to your father. Serve the Lord in truth, and seek to do the things that please him.
اِسْمَعُوا يَا بَنِيَّ لأَبِيكُمُ، اعْبُدُوا الرَّبَّ بِحَقٍّ، وَابْتَغُوا عَمَلَ مَرْضَاتِهِ،
And command your sons, so that they may accomplish justice and almsgiving, and so that they may be mindful of God and may bless him at all times, in truth and with all their strength.
And command your sons, so that they may accomplish justice and almsgiving, and so that they may be mindful of God and may bless him at all times, in truth and with all their strength.
وَأَوْصُوا بَنِيكُمْ بِعَمَلِ الْعَدْلِ وَالصَّدَقَاتِ، وَأَنْ يَذْكُرُوا اللهَ وَيُبَارِكُوهُ كُلَّ حِينٍ بِالْحَقِّ وَبِكُلِّ طَاقَتِهِمْ.
And now, sons, listen to me, and do not remain here. But, on whatever day you will bury your mother near me in one sepulcher, from that time, direct your steps to leave this place.
And now, sons, listen to me, and do not remain here. But, on whatever day you will bury your mother near me in one sepulcher, from that time, direct your steps to leave this place.
اِسْمَعُوا لِي يَا بَنِيَّ، لاَ تُقِيمُوا ههُنَا، بَلْ أَيَّ يَوْمٍ دَفَنْتُمْ وَالِدَتَكُمْ مَعِي فِي قَبْرٍ وَاحِدٍ فَفِي ذلِكَ الْيَوْمِ وَجِّهُوا خَطَوَاتِكُمْ لِلْخُرُوجِ مِنْ هذَا الْمَوْضِعِ،
For I see that its iniquity will bring about its end.”
For I see that its iniquity will bring about its end.”
فَإِنِّي أَرَى أَنَّ إِثْمَهُ سَيُهْلِكُهُ».
And it happened that, after the death of his mother, Tobias withdrew from Nineveh, with his wife, and sons, and sons of sons, and he was returned to his father-in-law.
And it happened that, after the death of his mother, Tobias withdrew from Nineveh, with his wife, and sons, and sons of sons, and he was returned to his father-in-law.
فَكَانَ أَنَّ طُوبِيَّا بَعْدَ مَوْتِ أُمِّهِ، ارْتَحَلَ عَنْ نِينَوَى بِزَوْجَتِهِ وَبَنِيهِ وَبَنِي بَنِيهِ، وَرَجَعَ إِلَى حَمَوَيْهِ.

And he found them unharmed in a good old age. And he took care of them, and he closed their eyes. And all the inheritance of the house of Raguel passed to him. And he saw the sons of his sons to the fifth generation.

And he found them unharmed in a good old age. And he took care of them, and he closed their eyes. And all the inheritance of the house of Raguel passed to him. And he saw the sons of his sons to the fifth generation.
فَوَجَدَهُمَا سَالِمَيْنِ بِشَيْخُوخَةٍ صَالِحَةٍ فَاهْتَمَّ بِهِمَا، وَهُوَ أَغْمَضَ أَعْيُنَهُمَا، وَأَحْرَزَ كُلَّ مِيرَاثِ بَيْتِ رَعُوئِيلَ، وَرَأَى بَنِي بَنِيهِ إِلَى الْجِيلِ الْخَامِسِ.
And, having completed ninety-nine years in the fear of the Lord, with joy, they buried him.
And, having completed ninety-nine years in the fear of the Lord, with joy, they buried him.
وَبَعْدَ أَنِ اسْتَوْفَى تِسْعاً وَتِسْعِينَ سَنَةً فِي مَخَافَةِ الرَّبِّ، دُفِنَ بِفَرَحٍ.
But all his family and all his lineage continued with a good life and in holy conversation, so that they were acceptable both to God and to men, as well as to everyone who dwelt in the land.
But all his family and all his lineage continued with a good life and in holy conversation, so that they were acceptable both to God and to men, as well as to everyone who dwelt in the land.
وَلَبِثَ كُلُّ ذَوِي قَرَابَتِهِ وَجَمِيعُ أَعْقَابِهِ فِي عِيشَةٍ صَالِحَةٍ وَسِيرَةٍ مُقَدَّسَةٍ، وَكَانُوا مَرْضِيِّينَ لَدَى اللهِ وَالنَّاسِ وَجَمِيعِ سُكَّانِ الأَرْضِ.
